proposition_text: These Regulations may be cited as the Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2015.
short_name: Citation
label: Citation

proposition_text: These Regulations come into force on 1st May 2015.
short_name: Commencement date
label: Commencement date

proposition_text: These Regulations apply in relation to England only.
short_name: Territorial application
label: Territorial application

proposition_text: Parts 3 to 8 apply only in relation to holdings that are in nitrate vulnerable zones designated for the purposes of these Regulations.
short_name: Application to nitrate vulnerable zones
label: Application to nitrate vulnerable zones

proposition_text: Slurry is defined as excreta produced by livestock (other than poultry) while in a yard or building (including any bedding, rainwater or washings mixed with it) that has a consistency that allows it to be pumped or discharged by gravity; in the case of excreta separated into its liquid and solid fractions, the slurry is the liquid fraction.
short_name: Definition of slurry
label: Definition of slurry

proposition_text: Organic manure is defined as a nitrogen fertiliser or phosphate fertiliser derived from animal, plant or human sources and includes livestock manure.
short_name: Definition of organic manure
label: Definition of organic manure

proposition_text: Agricultural has the meaning given by section 109(3) of the Agriculture Act 1947.
short_name: Definition of agricultural (cross-reference)
label: Definition of agricultural (cross-reference)

proposition_text: Spreading, in relation to land, includes applying to the surface of the land, injecting into the land or mixing with the surface layers of the land, but does not include the direct deposit of excreta on to land by animals.
short_name: Definition of spreading
label: Definition of spreading

proposition_text: An area is designated as a nitrate vulnerable zone if it is land that drains into polluted waters, contributes to the pollution of those waters, and is marked as such a zone on a relevant map.
short_name: Designation of nitrate vulnerable zones
label: Designation of nitrate vulnerable zones

proposition_text: For the period from 1 December 2016 until the Secretary of State next revises or adds to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones under regulation 4(5), 'relevant map' means a map marked 'Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (England) 2017 to 2020' published on 1 December 2016 on the Agency's website.
short_name: Definition of relevant map (initial period)
label: Definition of relevant map (initial period)

proposition_text: Following any subsequent review under regulation 4(5), 'relevant map' means a map published on the Agency's website that is marked 'This map identifies those areas of England designated by the Secretary of State as a Nitrate Vulnerable Zone for the purposes of the Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2015 (England)' and specifies the period to which it relates.
short_name: Definition of relevant map (post-review)
label: Definition of relevant map (post-review)

proposition_text: The Agency must make provision for a relevant map to be obtainable by persons without access to the internet.
short_name: Duty to provide offline access to maps
label: Duty to provide offline access to maps

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must keep under review the eutrophic state of fresh surface waters, estuarial waters and coastal waters.
short_name: Duty to review eutrophic state of waters
label: Duty to review eutrophic state of waters

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must, before 1st January 2017 and before 1st January of every fourth year thereafter, monitor the nitrate concentration in freshwaters over a period of one year at sampling stations representative of surface water at least monthly and more frequently during flood periods.
short_name: Duty to monitor nitrate concentration in surface water
label: Duty to monitor nitrate concentration in surface water

proposition_text: No later than the end of each four-year period, the Secretary of State must identify water affected by pollution or that could be if controls are not applied, identify land draining into those waters that contributes to pollution, take into account changes and factors unforeseen at the time of previous designation, and if necessary revise or add to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones.
short_name: Duty to identify polluted waters and designate nitrate vulnerable zones
label: Duty to identify polluted waters and designate nitrate vulnerable zones

proposition_text: The Agency must make recommendations to the Secretary of State before 10th August 2016 and before 10th August of every fourth year thereafter as to which areas of land should be, or should continue to be, designated as nitrate vulnerable zones, by reference to the matters in regulation 4(5)(a) to (c).
short_name: Agency duty: quadrennial NVZ recommendations
label: Agency duty: quadrennial NVZ recommendations

proposition_text: In deciding whether to revise or add to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones for the purposes of regulation 4(5)(d), the Secretary of State must have regard to the recommendations made by the Agency under regulation 5(1).
short_name: Secretary of State duty: regard to Agency recommendations
label: Secretary of State duty: regard to Agency recommendations

proposition_text: Before revising or adding to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones (except where paragraph (3A) applies), the Secretary of State must publish the proposals to revise or add to the designation and send written notice to any person appearing to be the owner or occupier of a relevant holding.
short_name: Secretary of State duty: publication and notice (general)
label: Secretary of State duty: publication and notice (general)

proposition_text: Where the Secretary of State revises or adds to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones before 1st January 2017, the Secretary of State must by 31st December 2016 publish the proposals on the Agency's website and by 1st March 2017 send written notice to any person appearing to be the owner or occupier of a relevant holding.
short_name: Secretary of State duty: transitional publication and notice (pre-2017)
label: Secretary of State duty: transitional publication and notice (pre-2017)

proposition_text: The requirements of the regulations listed in Schedule 4 do not apply in relation to a new holding until the dates set out in that Schedule.
short_name: Transitional exemption for new holdings from Schedule 4 requirements
label: Transitional exemption for new holdings from Schedule 4 requirements

proposition_text: An owner or occupier of a relevant holding who is sent a notice under regulation 5(3)(b) or 5(3A)(b) may appeal to the First-tier Tribunal against the proposals or designation referred to in the notice.
short_name: Right of appeal to First-tier Tribunal for owners/occupiers of relevant holdings
label: Right of appeal to First-tier Tribunal for owners/occupiers of relevant holdings

proposition_text: The only grounds of appeal under regulation 6 are that the relevant holding (or any part of it) does not drain into water which the Secretary of State proposes to identify, or to continue to identify, as polluted or which has been similarly identified in Wales or Scotland, or in a case within regulation 5(3A), does not drain into water which the Secretary of State has identified as polluted or which has been similarly identified in Wales or Scotland.
short_name: First ground of appeal: holding does not drain into polluted water
label: First ground of appeal: holding does not drain into polluted water

proposition_text: The only grounds of appeal under regulation 6 include that the relevant holding (or any part of it) drains into water which the Secretary of State should not identify, or should not continue to identify, as polluted.
short_name: Second ground of appeal: water should not be identified as polluted
label: Second ground of appeal: water should not be identified as polluted

proposition_text: If the First-tier Tribunal upholds an appeal under paragraph (2)(b), the Secretary of State, when acting under regulation 4(5), must treat the water concerned as water which should not be identified, or should not continue to be identified, as polluted, and treat any holding (or part of any holding) which drains into that water accordingly regardless of whether the owner or occupier of the relevant holding appealed.
short_name: Effect of successful appeal on ground (2)(b): water de-designation and consequential treatment of all holdings
label: Effect of successful appeal on ground (2)(b): water de-designation and consequential treatment of all holdings

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must ensure that the total amount of nitrogen in livestock manure applied to the holding in any calendar year does not exceed 170kg multiplied by the area of the holding in hectares.
short_name: Annual nitrogen limit from livestock manure
label: Annual nitrogen limit from livestock manure

proposition_text: The nitrogen limit does not apply where the occupier has been granted a derogation.
short_name: Derogation exception to nitrogen limit
label: Derogation exception to nitrogen limit

proposition_text: In calculating the area of a holding for nitrogen spreading limits, no account is to be taken of surface waters, hardstanding, buildings, roads, or woodland (unless used for grazing).
short_name: Exclusion of certain land types from area calculation
label: Exclusion of certain land types from area calculation

proposition_text: In calculating the area of a holding for nitrogen spreading limits, no account is to be taken of land covered by a greenhouse for the whole of the year concerned.
short_name: Exclusion of year-round greenhouse land from area calculation
label: Exclusion of year-round greenhouse land from area calculation

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must ensure that in any twelve-month period, the total amount of nitrogen in organic manure spread on any given hectare of land on the holding does not exceed 250kg.
short_name: Nitrogen limit in organic manure: 250kg per hectare per year
label: Nitrogen limit in organic manure: 250kg per hectare per year

proposition_text: The occupier may exceed the 250kg nitrogen limit if organic manure is in the form of certified green compost or certified green/food compost, the total nitrogen in such compost does not exceed 500kg per hectare in any two-year period (or 1,000kg per hectare in any four-year period for orchard land spread as mulch), and no other form of organic manure is spread over the land during the applicable period.
short_name: Exception for certified green compost: higher nitrogen limits apply
label: Exception for certified green compost: higher nitrogen limits apply

proposition_text: Regulation 8 does not apply where at least 80% of the holding's agricultural area is sown with grass, the total nitrogen in organic manure applied to the holding does not exceed 100kg multiplied by the area in hectares, the total nitrogen from manufactured nitrogen fertiliser does not exceed 90kg multiplied by the area in hectares, the occupier does not bring organic manure onto the holding, and the occupier makes a record demonstrating these requirements are met.
short_name: Grassland exemption: alternative nitrogen regime for holdings with 80%+ grass
label: Grassland exemption: alternative nitrogen regime for holdings with 80%+ grass

proposition_text: For the purposes of regulation 8, the total amount of nitrogen in organic manure is to be calculated by reference to the methods described in regulation 14, and the reference to land on the holding does not include any land which is covered by a greenhouse for the whole of the period concerned.
short_name: Calculation method and greenhouse exclusion for nitrogen limits
label: Calculation method and greenhouse exclusion for nitrogen limits

proposition_text: Part 3 does not apply to the spreading of nitrogen fertiliser on a crop growing in a greenhouse.
short_name: Exemption: nitrogen fertiliser spreading in greenhouses
label: Exemption: nitrogen fertiliser spreading in greenhouses

proposition_text: Before spreading nitrogen fertiliser, the occupier of a holding must calculate the amount of nitrogen in the soil likely to be available for crop uptake during the growing season, calculate the optimum amount of nitrogen to be spread taking that soil nitrogen into account, and produce a fertilisation plan.
short_name: Duty to calculate soil nitrogen, optimum nitrogen amount, and produce fertilisation plan before spreading
label: Duty to calculate soil nitrogen, optimum nitrogen amount, and produce fertilisation plan before spreading

proposition_text: For permanent grassland, the occupier must comply with the fertilisation planning duty each calendar year before the first spreading of nitrogen fertiliser.
short_name: Annual fertilisation planning duty for permanent grassland
label: Annual fertilisation planning duty for permanent grassland

proposition_text: For any crop other than permanent grassland, the occupier must comply with the fertilisation planning duty before spreading nitrogen fertiliser for the first time for a crop planted or intended to be planted.
short_name: Fertilisation planning duty for non-permanent grassland crops
label: Fertilisation planning duty for non-permanent grassland crops

proposition_text: The fertilisation plan must be in permanent form and must record the field reference or name, the area planted or intended to be planted, the crop type, soil type, previous crop details, the calculated soil nitrogen amount and calculation method, expected planting month, expected yield (if arable), and the optimum nitrogen amount.
short_name: Mandatory content and form requirements for fertilisation plans
label: Mandatory content and form requirements for fertilisation plans

proposition_text: Before spreading organic manure, the occupier of a holding must on each occasion calculate the amount of nitrogen that is likely to be available from that manure for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Calculation of available nitrogen before spreading organic manure
label: Calculation of available nitrogen before spreading organic manure

proposition_text: Before spreading organic manure, the occupier of a holding must record the area on which the manure is to be spread, the quantity to be spread, the month in which it is to be spread, the type of manure, its total nitrogen content, and the amount of nitrogen likely to be available from the manure to be spread for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Recording requirements before spreading organic manure
label: Recording requirements before spreading organic manure

proposition_text: Before spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser, the occupier of a holding must record the amount required and the month in which it is planned to spread it.
short_name: Recording requirements before spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser
label: Recording requirements before spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding may, acting on written advice of a FACTS adviser, spread nitrogen on grass grown for minimum 16% protein content in amounts exceeding regulation 12 limits, subject to absolute caps.
short_name: Derogation for high-protein grass: FACTS-advised nitrogen spreading
label: Derogation for high-protein grass: FACTS-advised nitrogen spreading

proposition_text: The total amount of nitrogen spread on any given hectare of high-protein grass must not exceed 700 kg if irrigated or 500 kg if not irrigated.
short_name: Absolute nitrogen caps for high-protein grass
label: Absolute nitrogen caps for high-protein grass

proposition_text: For high-protein grass nitrogen spreading, the total amount of nitrogen from organic manure must be calculated by reference to the methods for establishing available nitrogen described in regulation 14.
short_name: Calculation method for organic manure nitrogen
label: Calculation method for organic manure nitrogen

proposition_text: After relying on the high-protein grass derogation, the occupier must provide the FACTS adviser with soil analyses from representative autumn soil samples (taken 1 September to 31 October) so the adviser may take them into account when advising for subsequent years.
short_name: Post-spreading soil analysis obligation
label: Post-spreading soil analysis obligation

proposition_text: For livestock manure, nitrogen content must be calculated either by using the standard table in Part 1 of Schedule 2 or by sampling and analysis in accordance with Part 2 of that Schedule.
short_name: Livestock manure nitrogen content calculation methods
label: Livestock manure nitrogen content calculation methods

proposition_text: For cattle slurry spread on or after 1st January 2014, the available nitrogen is 40% of the nitrogen content.
short_name: Cattle slurry available nitrogen percentage (post-2014)
label: Cattle slurry available nitrogen percentage (post-2014)

proposition_text: For pig slurry spread on or after 1st January 2014, the available nitrogen is 50% of the nitrogen content.
short_name: Pig slurry available nitrogen percentage (post-2014)
label: Pig slurry available nitrogen percentage (post-2014)

proposition_text: An occupier may not spread organic manure on a crop referred to in regulation 12 or 13 if no nitrogen value is established for that manure for the purposes of regulations 8(9), 12(2), 13(4) or 31(6).
short_name: Prohibition on spreading organic manure without established nitrogen values
label: Prohibition on spreading organic manure without established nitrogen values

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who spreads organic manure on that holding must produce and maintain a risk map showing each field with its area in hectares, all surface waters, any boreholes, springs or wells on the holding or within 50 metres of the boundary, areas with sandy or shallow soils, land with an incline greater than 12 degrees, land within 10 metres of surface waters, land within 50 metres of a borehole, spring or well, land drains (other than sealed impermeable pipes), sites suitable for temporary field heaps if this storage method is to be used, and land that has a low run-off risk.
short_name: Duty to produce and maintain risk map for organic manure spreading
label: Duty to produce and maintain risk map for organic manure spreading

proposition_text: Information on land that has a low run-off risk need not be included in a risk map produced by an occupier who does not intend to spread manure on such land during the storage period referred to in regulation 25.
short_name: Exception for low run-off risk land information in risk map
label: Exception for low run-off risk land information in risk map

proposition_text: Where a change in circumstances affects a matter referred to in the risk map requirements, the occupier must update the risk map within three months of the change.
short_name: Duty to update risk map within three months of relevant change
label: Duty to update risk map within three months of relevant change

proposition_text: The occupier must keep a copy of the risk map for the holding.
short_name: Duty to retain copy of risk map
label: Duty to retain copy of risk map

proposition_text: Before spreading nitrogen fertiliser, the occupier of a holding must undertake a field inspection to consider the risk of nitrogen getting into surface water.
short_name: Pre-spreading field inspection duty
label: Pre-spreading field inspection duty

proposition_text: A person must not spread nitrogen fertiliser on land if there is a significant risk of nitrogen getting into surface water, taking into account slope (especially if greater than 12 degrees), ground cover, proximity to surface water, weather conditions, soil type, and presence of land drains.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading where significant risk to surface water
label: Prohibition on spreading where significant risk to surface water

proposition_text: A person must not spread nitrogen fertiliser if the soil is waterlogged, flooded, snow covered, or has been frozen for more than 12 hours in the previous 24 hours.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading in adverse soil conditions
label: Prohibition on spreading in adverse soil conditions

proposition_text: A person must not spread manufactured nitrogen fertiliser within 2 metres of surface water.
short_name: 2-metre buffer zone for manufactured nitrogen fertiliser
label: 2-metre buffer zone for manufactured nitrogen fertiliser

proposition_text: A person must not spread organic manure within 10 metres of surface water, except where permitted by specific exceptions for livestock manure or slurry/sewage sludge/anaerobic digestate with appropriate equipment.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 10 metres of surface water
label: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 10 metres of surface water

proposition_text: Livestock manure (other than slurry or poultry manure) may be spread within 10 metres of surface water if it is spread on land managed for breeding wader birds or as species-rich semi-natural grassland that is notified as a site of special scientific interest or subject to an agri-environmental commitment, during the period 1st June to 31st October, not directly on surface water, and the total annual amount does not exceed 12.5 tonnes per hectare.
short_name: Exception for livestock manure on designated conservation land
label: Exception for livestock manure on designated conservation land

proposition_text: Organic manure in the form of slurry, sewage sludge or anaerobic digestate may be spread within 10 metres but not within 6 metres of surface water if the spreading equipment used is a trailing hose band spreader, a trailing shoe band spreader, an injector which injects the organic manure no deeper than 10cm below the surface of the soil, or a dribble bar applicator.
short_name: Exception for slurry, sewage sludge and anaerobic digestate with specified equipment
label: Exception for slurry, sewage sludge and anaerobic digestate with specified equipment

proposition_text: A person must not spread organic manure within 50 metres of a borehole, spring or well.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 50 metres of water sources
label: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 50 metres of water sources

proposition_text: Equipment used to spread slurry by broadcasting must have a trajectory of less than 4 metres from the ground.
short_name: Slurry broadcasting equipment: low-trajectory requirement
label: Slurry broadcasting equipment: low-trajectory requirement

proposition_text: Equipment used to spread slurry by broadcasting that has a trajectory of 4 metres or more from the ground must be able to achieve a maximum slurry application rate of 1 mm per hour when operating continuously.
short_name: Slurry broadcasting equipment: high-trajectory application rate limit
label: Slurry broadcasting equipment: high-trajectory application rate limit

proposition_text: A person who applies organic manure on to the surface of bare soil or stubble (other than soil that has been sown) must ensure that it is incorporated into the soil in accordance with this regulation.
short_name: Duty to incorporate organic manure applied to bare soil or stubble
label: Duty to incorporate organic manure applied to bare soil or stubble

proposition_text: Poultry manure applied to bare soil or stubble must be incorporated as soon as practicable and within 24 hours at the latest.
short_name: Incorporation timeframe for poultry manure
label: Incorporation timeframe for poultry manure

proposition_text: Slurry or liquid from the treatment of sewage sludge by anaerobic digestion must be incorporated as soon as practicable and within 24 hours at the latest, unless it was applied using equipment described in regulation 17(4)(a) or (c) or an injector which injects the organic manure into the ground.
short_name: Incorporation timeframe for slurry and liquid digestate
label: Incorporation timeframe for slurry and liquid digestate

proposition_text: Any other organic manure (other than organic manure spread as a mulch on sandy soil) must be incorporated as soon as practicable and within 24 hours at the latest, if the land is within 50 metres of surface water and slopes in such a way that there may be a run-off to that water.
short_name: Incorporation timeframe for other organic manure near surface water on sloping land
label: Incorporation timeframe for other organic manure near surface water on sloping land

proposition_text: A person must not spread organic manure with high readily available nitrogen on land during the closed period.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading organic manure with high readily available nitrogen during closed period
label: Prohibition on spreading organic manure with high readily available nitrogen during closed period

proposition_text: A person may spread organic manure with high readily available nitrogen on tillage land with sandy or shallow soil during the period from 1st August to 15th September if the next crop sown on the land is sown on or before 15th September.
short_name: Exception for tillage land with sandy or shallow soil (1 Aug–15 Sep)
label: Exception for tillage land with sandy or shallow soil (1 Aug–15 Sep)

proposition_text: Between the end of the closed period and the end of the following February, the maximum amount of slurry that may be spread at any one time is 30 cubic metres per hectare.
short_name: Maximum slurry spreading limit (30 m³/ha) post-closed period
label: Maximum slurry spreading limit (30 m³/ha) post-closed period

proposition_text: Between the end of the closed period and the end of the following February, there must be at least three weeks between any spreading of slurry or poultry manure.
short_name: Minimum three-week interval between spreading events post-closed period
label: Minimum three-week interval between spreading events post-closed period

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding under the organic control system may spread organic manure with high readily available nitrogen on winter oilseed rape, asparagus, brassica, grass, over-wintered salad onions, parsley or bulb onions during the closed period, or on any other crop if in accordance with written advice from a FACTS adviser.
short_name: Organic holdings: derogation for spreading high readily available nitrogen manure on specified crops
label: Organic holdings: derogation for spreading high readily available nitrogen manure on specified crops

proposition_text: In the case of winter oilseed rape under the organic holding derogation, nitrogen must not be spread after 31st October.
short_name: Winter oilseed rape: nitrogen spreading deadline
label: Winter oilseed rape: nitrogen spreading deadline

proposition_text: In the case of brassica under the organic holding derogation, an additional 50kg of nitrogen per hectare may be spread every four weeks during the closed period up to harvest, provided the 150kg total nitrogen limit is not exceeded.
short_name: Brassica: additional nitrogen allowance during closed period
label: Brassica: additional nitrogen allowance during closed period

proposition_text: In the case of grass under the organic holding derogation, nitrogen must not be spread after 31st October and a maximum of 40kg of nitrogen per hectare may be spread at any one time.
short_name: Grass: nitrogen spreading deadline and single-application limit
label: Grass: nitrogen spreading deadline and single-application limit

proposition_text: A person must not spread manufactured nitrogen fertiliser on land during the closed period.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser during closed period
label: Prohibition on spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser during closed period

proposition_text: The closed period for grassland begins with 15th September and ends with the following 15th January.
short_name: Closed period definition for grassland
label: Closed period definition for grassland

proposition_text: The closed period for tillage land begins with 1st September and ends with the following 15th January.
short_name: Closed period definition for tillage land
label: Closed period definition for tillage land

proposition_text: A person may spread fertiliser during the closed period on a crop specified in the table if the rate of nitrogen specified in the second column is not exceeded.
short_name: Exception for specified crops during closed period
label: Exception for specified crops during closed period

proposition_text: A heap of solid manure forming a temporary field site must occupy as small a surface area as is practically required to support the mass of the heap and prevent it from collapse.
short_name: Surface area requirement for solid manure heaps on temporary field sites
label: Surface area requirement for solid manure heaps on temporary field sites

proposition_text: Solid poultry manure that does not have bedding mixed into it and is stored on a temporary field site must be covered with an impermeable material.
short_name: Covering requirement for solid poultry manure on temporary field sites
label: Covering requirement for solid poultry manure on temporary field sites

proposition_text: The occupier of a relevant holding must separate slurry mechanically or on an impermeable surface where the liquid drains into a suitable receptacle.
short_name: Slurry separation method requirements
label: Slurry separation method requirements

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who keeps specified animals must provide sufficient storage for all slurry produced on the holding during the storage period and for all poultry manure produced in a yard or building on the holding during the storage period.
short_name: Duty to provide slurry and poultry manure storage
label: Duty to provide slurry and poultry manure storage

proposition_text: A slurry store must have the capacity to store, in addition to the manure, any rainfall, washings or other liquid that enters the vessel (either directly or indirectly) during the storage period.
short_name: Slurry store capacity for additional liquids
label: Slurry store capacity for additional liquids

proposition_text: Storage facilities are not necessary for slurry or poultry manure that the occupier intends will be sent off the holding or spread in accordance with these Regulations on land that has a low run-off risk.
short_name: Exception to storage requirement for off-holding or low run-off spreading
label: Exception to storage requirement for off-holding or low run-off spreading

proposition_text: Where slurry or poultry manure is intended to be spread on land with a low run-off risk, storage facilities for an additional one week's manure must be provided as a contingency measure in the event of spreading not being possible on some dates.
short_name: Contingency storage for low run-off spreading
label: Contingency storage for low run-off spreading

proposition_text: The occupier of a new holding must record the total size of the holding, calculated in accordance with regulation 7(4).
short_name: Duty to record total size of new holding
label: Duty to record total size of new holding

proposition_text: The occupier of a new holding with livestock must calculate and record the amount of manure that will be produced by the number of animals expected to be kept in a building or on hardstanding during the storage period, using the figures in Schedule 1.
short_name: Calculation and recording of manure production for new holdings
label: Calculation and recording of manure production for new holdings

proposition_text: The occupier of a new holding with livestock must calculate and record the amount of storage capacity (in slurry vessels and hardstanding) required to enable compliance with regulation 25, taking into account the amount of manure intended to be exported from the holding, the amount of manure intended to be spread on land that has a low run-off risk, and in the case of a slurry vessel, the amount of liquids other than slurry likely to enter the vessel.
short_name: Calculation and recording of required storage capacity for new holdings
label: Calculation and recording of required storage capacity for new holdings

proposition_text: The occupier of a new holding with livestock must calculate and record the current storage capacity of the holding.
short_name: Recording of current storage capacity for new holdings
label: Recording of current storage capacity for new holdings

proposition_text: Before 30th April each year, the occupier of a holding with livestock must record for the previous storage period the number of animals in a building or hardstanding on the holding during that period and the category for each animal by reference to Schedule 1.
short_name: Annual livestock record-keeping obligation
label: Annual livestock record-keeping obligation

proposition_text: The occupier must also record the temporary field sites used for free-standing heaps as referred to in regulation 23 and the dates of use.
short_name: Temporary field site record-keeping obligation
label: Temporary field site record-keeping obligation

proposition_text: In regulation 26, 'storage period' has the meaning given in regulation 25(7).
short_name: Definition of storage period for record-keeping
label: Definition of storage period for record-keeping

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding with livestock must, before 30th April each year, record the number of animals on the holding during the previous calendar year and the category for each animal by reference to Schedule 1, and the number of days that each animal spent on the holding.
short_name: Annual livestock record-keeping obligation
label: Annual livestock record-keeping obligation

proposition_text: The occupier must calculate the amount of nitrogen in the manure produced by the animals on the holding during that year by reference to Schedule 1.
short_name: Nitrogen calculation obligation
label: Nitrogen calculation obligation

proposition_text: In the case of permanently housed pigs or poultry, the occupier may use software approved by the Secretary of State or, in the case of a system of keeping livestock that only produces solid manure, sampling and analysis in accordance with Part 2 of Schedule 2, as an alternative to the method referred to in paragraph (2).
short_name: Alternative nitrogen calculation methods for permanently housed pigs or poultry
label: Alternative nitrogen calculation methods for permanently housed pigs or poultry

proposition_text: The occupier must make a record of the calculations and how the final figures were arrived at, and where the occupier uses software approved by the Secretary of State, the occupier must keep a printout of the result.
short_name: Record-keeping of nitrogen calculations and software outputs
label: Record-keeping of nitrogen calculations and software outputs

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who brings livestock manure onto the holding must, within one week, record the type and amount of livestock manure, the date it was brought on, the nitrogen content (if known), and the name and address of the supplier.
short_name: Record-keeping for livestock manure brought onto holding
label: Record-keeping for livestock manure brought onto holding

proposition_text: An occupier who sends livestock manure off a holding must, within one week, record the type and amount of livestock manure, the date it was sent off, the nitrogen content, the name and address of the recipient, and details of a contingency plan in the event that an agreement for a person to accept the livestock manure fails.
short_name: Record-keeping for livestock manure sent off holding
label: Record-keeping for livestock manure sent off holding

proposition_text: If the nitrogen content of livestock manure brought onto a holding is not known, the occupier must ascertain it as soon as reasonably practicable after arrival and record it within one week of ascertaining it.
short_name: Duty to ascertain and record unknown nitrogen content
label: Duty to ascertain and record unknown nitrogen content

proposition_text: For the purposes of ascertaining nitrogen content, it must be determined either by using the standard figures in Part 1 of Schedule 2 or by sampling and analysis as set out in Part 2 of that Schedule.
short_name: Methods for ascertaining nitrogen content
label: Methods for ascertaining nitrogen content

proposition_text: A person using sampling and analysis to determine nitrogen content in organic manure must keep the original laboratory report.
short_name: Duty to retain original laboratory report for nitrogen content analysis in organic manure
label: Duty to retain original laboratory report for nitrogen content analysis in organic manure

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must, within one week of sowing a crop on which nitrogen fertiliser is spread or intended to be spread, record the crop sown and the date of sowing.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for nitrogen fertiliser application on crops
label: Record-keeping obligation for nitrogen fertiliser application on crops

proposition_text: The record-keeping obligation for crops on which nitrogen fertiliser is spread does not apply to a crop growing in a greenhouse.
short_name: Greenhouse exception to crop record-keeping
label: Greenhouse exception to crop record-keeping

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding who spreads organic manure must, within one week of doing so, record the area spread, the quantity of organic manure spread, the date, the method, the type of organic manure, the total nitrogen content, and the amount of nitrogen that was available to the crop.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for organic manure spreading
label: Record-keeping obligation for organic manure spreading

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must, within one week of spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser, record the date of spreading and the amount of nitrogen spread.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for manufactured nitrogen fertiliser spreading
label: Record-keeping obligation for manufactured nitrogen fertiliser spreading

proposition_text: Regulation 31 does not apply to nitrogen fertiliser spread in a greenhouse.
short_name: Greenhouse exception to record-keeping obligations
label: Greenhouse exception to record-keeping obligations

proposition_text: Regulation 31 does not apply in a calendar year where at least 80% of the holding's agricultural area is sown with grass, the total nitrogen in organic manure applied does not exceed 100kg multiplied by the area in hectares, the total nitrogen from manufactured fertiliser does not exceed 90kg multiplied by the area in hectares, the occupier does not bring organic manure onto the holding, and the occupier records that these requirements are met.
short_name: Low-intensity farming exception to record-keeping obligations
label: Low-intensity farming exception to record-keeping obligations

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding who has used nitrogen fertiliser must record the yield achieved by an arable crop within one week of ascertaining it.
short_name: Duty to record arable crop yield after nitrogen fertiliser use
label: Duty to record arable crop yield after nitrogen fertiliser use

proposition_text: Before 30th April each year, the occupier of a holding must record how any grassland was managed in the previous calendar year.
short_name: Annual duty to record grassland management
label: Annual duty to record grassland management

proposition_text: The recording obligations in regulation 32 do not apply to crops in a greenhouse.
short_name: Greenhouse crops exemption from recording duties
label: Greenhouse crops exemption from recording duties

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who has relied on advice from a FACTS adviser for any purpose under these Regulations must keep a copy of that advice.
short_name: Duty to retain copy of FACTS adviser advice
label: Duty to retain copy of FACTS adviser advice

proposition_text: Where the size of a holding changes, the occupier must update the record required by regulation 35(1) of the old Regulations or regulation 25A within one month, but only insofar as there is a change in the area of land not covered by a greenhouse.
short_name: Duty to update holding-size record within one month when non-greenhouse land area changes
label: Duty to update holding-size record within one month when non-greenhouse land area changes

proposition_text: Where the amount of storage capacity of a holding changes, the occupier must update the record required by regulation 36(1)(b) of the old Regulations or regulation 25B within one week.
short_name: Duty to update storage-capacity record within one week when capacity changes
label: Duty to update storage-capacity record within one week when capacity changes

proposition_text: If the occupier introduces animals on to the holding for the first time, the occupier must within one month calculate and record the amount of manure that will be produced by the number of animals expected to be kept in a building or on hardstanding during the storage period, using the figures given in Schedule 1.
short_name: Duty to calculate and record expected manure production within one month of first introducing animals
label: Duty to calculate and record expected manure production within one month of first introducing animals

proposition_text: A person required to make a record under these Regulations must keep the record for 5 years after the record ceased to be current, regardless of whether the land has ceased to be designated as a nitrate vulnerable zone.
short_name: Record retention period: 5 years after record ceases to be current
label: Record retention period: 5 years after record ceases to be current

proposition_text: The reference to a record required to be made under these Regulations includes a record required to be made under the Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2008 in relation to which regulation 45 of those Regulations had effect immediately before commencement of these Regulations.
short_name: Transitional provision: records under 2008 Regulations included
label: Transitional provision: records under 2008 Regulations included

proposition_text: The Agency must determine an application for derogation as soon as practicable and notify the applicant in writing of its determination, giving reasons if the application is refused.
short_name: Determination and notification of derogation application
label: Determination and notification of derogation application

proposition_text: The Agency must refuse a derogation application if granting it would have an adverse effect on the integrity of a European site or European offshore marine site that has been subject to an appropriate assessment under regulation 63 of the Conservation of Habitats and Species Regulations 2017.
short_name: Mandatory refusal for adverse effect on European sites
label: Mandatory refusal for adverse effect on European sites

proposition_text: The Agency must refuse a derogation application if the occupier has been found in breach of the derogation conditions during the calendar year preceding that to which the application relates, subject to the exception where breach is found after application but before determination.
short_name: Mandatory refusal for prior breach of derogation conditions
label: Mandatory refusal for prior breach of derogation conditions

proposition_text: A person is regarded as having been found in breach of the derogation conditions if convicted of an offence under regulation 41 arising from breach of those conditions with no further appeal available or appeal decided against the person, or if the person has accepted a simple caution, had a penalty or notice imposed under regulation 42 with no further appeal or appeal decided against them, or given an enforcement undertaking under that regulation.
short_name: Definition of 'found in breach of derogation conditions'
label: Definition of 'found in breach of derogation conditions'

proposition_text: An occupier may appeal to an independent panel appointed by the Secretary of State against the Agency's refusal of an application under regulation 36.
short_name: Right to appeal refusal of derogation application
label: Right to appeal refusal of derogation application

proposition_text: An appeal under regulation 38 must be submitted before the end of 30 days beginning with the day after the date of the refusal.
short_name: Time limit for submitting appeal
label: Time limit for submitting appeal

proposition_text: If the panel allows an appeal under regulation 38, the Agency must designate the holding concerned as a derogated holding for the calendar year for which the application was made.
short_name: Consequence of successful appeal
label: Consequence of successful appeal

proposition_text: The panel must base its decision on documents submitted by the appellant and the Agency, and such additional information from the appellant or the Agency as it considers necessary.
short_name: Basis for panel decision
label: Basis for panel decision

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must review the effectiveness of nitrate vulnerable zone restrictions at least every four years and revise them if necessary.
short_name: Four-yearly review and revision of NVZ restrictions
label: Four-yearly review and revision of NVZ restrictions

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must establish a monitoring programme to assess the effectiveness of nitrate vulnerable zone restrictions.
short_name: Monitoring programme for NVZ restrictions
label: Monitoring programme for NVZ restrictions

proposition_text: When reviewing nitrate vulnerable zone restrictions, the Secretary of State must take into account available scientific and technical data (particularly nitrogen contributions from agricultural or other sources) and regional environmental conditions.
short_name: Factors for NVZ review
label: Factors for NVZ review

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must ensure the public are given early and effective opportunities to participate in the review, including consultation about proposals, access to relevant information, and opportunity to express comments before decisions are made.
short_name: Public participation in NVZ review
label: Public participation in NVZ review

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must from time to time carry out a review of the Water Protection (Nitrate Pollution Prevention) Regulations 2015, in so far as not already done under regulation 39.
short_name: Duty to review Regulations
label: Duty to review Regulations

proposition_text: The first report under regulation 40 must be published before 31st March 2020.
short_name: Deadline for first review report
label: Deadline for first review report

proposition_text: Subsequent reports under regulation 40 are to be published at intervals not exceeding 5 years.
short_name: Frequency of subsequent review reports
label: Frequency of subsequent review reports

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must prepare a report on the implementation of these Regulations for each relevant period of four years beginning with 1st January 2016 and each successive period of four years.
short_name: Duty to prepare implementation report every four years
label: Duty to prepare implementation report every four years

proposition_text: A report under regulation 40A(1) must contain details of any steps taken to promote good agricultural practice.
short_name: Report must contain details of steps to promote good agricultural practice
label: Report must contain details of steps to promote good agricultural practice

proposition_text: A report under regulation 40A(1) must contain the map published under regulation 3(2), accompanied by a statement detailing the nature of, and reasons for, any revisions to the designated nitrate vulnerable zone since the end of the previous reporting period.
short_name: Report must contain NVZ map with statement of revisions
label: Report must contain NVZ map with statement of revisions

proposition_text: A person who fails to comply with the requirements of regulations 7 and 8, regulations 10 to 13, regulations 15 to 35, or regulation 37(3) commits an offence.
short_name: Offence for non-compliance with specified regulations
label: Offence for non-compliance with specified regulations

proposition_text: A person guilty of an offence under regulation 41 is liable on summary conviction to a fine, or on conviction on indictment to a fine.
short_name: Penalties for offence under regulation 41
label: Penalties for offence under regulation 41

proposition_text: Where a body corporate commits an offence under regulation 41 with the consent, connivance or attributable neglect of a director, manager, secretary or similar officer, that person as well as the body corporate is guilty of the offence and liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly.
short_name: Corporate officer liability for body corporate offences
label: Corporate officer liability for body corporate offences

proposition_text: In regulation 41(3), 'director' in relation to a body corporate whose affairs are managed by its members means a member of the body.
short_name: Definition of 'director' for member-managed bodies corporate
label: Definition of 'director' for member-managed bodies corporate

proposition_text: The Agency may impose a fixed monetary penalty, variable monetary penalty, restoration notice, compliance notice or stop notice, or may accept an enforcement undertaking, in relation to an offence under regulation 41, as if it were an offence specified in Schedule 5 to the Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010.
short_name: Agency enforcement powers for regulation 41 offences
label: Agency enforcement powers for regulation 41 offences

proposition_text: An expression used in paragraph (1) and in the Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010 has the same meaning in that paragraph as it has in that Order.
short_name: Interpretive alignment with Environmental Civil Sanctions Order
label: Interpretive alignment with Environmental Civil Sanctions Order

proposition_text: The provisions of the Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010 relating to the sanctions referred to in paragraph (1) apply as if they were provisions of these Regulations.
short_name: Incorporation of Environmental Civil Sanctions Order provisions
label: Incorporation of Environmental Civil Sanctions Order provisions

proposition_text: The Agency has the function of enforcing these Regulations.
short_name: Agency enforcement function
label: Agency enforcement function

proposition_text: The Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2008 are revoked.
short_name: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2008
label: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2008

proposition_text: Regulations 2 to 25 of the Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) and Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (England) (Amendment) Regulations 2013 are revoked.
short_name: Revocation of regulations 2–25 of 2013 Amendment Regulations on slurry and agricultural fuel oil
label: Revocation of regulations 2–25 of 2013 Amendment Regulations on slurry and agricultural fuel oil

proposition_text: The Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) Regulations 2009 are revoked.
short_name: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) Regulations 2009
label: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) Regulations 2009

proposition_text: The Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) Regulations 2012 are revoked.
short_name: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) Regulations 2012
label: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Amendment) Regulations 2012

proposition_text: A dairy cow after first calf with an annual milk yield more than 9000 litres produces 64 litres of manure, 315 grams of nitrogen, and 142 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by high-yield dairy cow
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by high-yield dairy cow
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Grazing livestock table, Cattle – Dairy cow – After first calf and annual milk yield more than 9000 litres

proposition_text: A sow including litter up to 7kg fed on a diet supplemented with synthetic amino acids produces 10.9 litres of manure, 44 grams of nitrogen, and 37 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by sow with litter (amino acid-supplemented diet)
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by sow with litter (amino acid-supplemented diet)
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Non-grazing livestock table, Pigs – sow (including litter up to 7kg) fed on a diet supplemented with synthetic amino acids

proposition_text: A sow including litter up to 7kg fed on a diet without synthetic amino acids produces 10.9 litres of manure, 49 grams of nitrogen, and 37 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by sow with litter (non-supplemented diet)
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by sow with litter (non-supplemented diet)
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Non-grazing livestock table, Pigs – sow (including litter up to 7kg) fed on a diet without synthetic amino acids

proposition_text: Figures for a ewe include one or more suckled lambs until the lambs are aged 6 months.
short_name: Ewe manure figures include suckled lambs up to 6 months
label: Ewe manure figures include suckled lambs up to 6 months

proposition_text: Slurry from cattle contains 2.6 kg of total nitrogen per cubic metre.
short_name: Nitrogen content in cattle slurry
label: Nitrogen content in cattle slurry

proposition_text: At least five samples, each of two litres, must be taken in the case of slurry and other liquid and semi-liquid organic manure.
short_name: Slurry sampling requirement - number and volume
label: Slurry sampling requirement - number and volume
fragment_locator: Schedule 2, Part 2, Slurry and other liquid and semi-liquid organic manure, paragraph 1

proposition_text: Slurry or other manure must, if it is reasonably practicable to do so, be thoroughly mixed before the samples are taken; and each sample must be taken from a different location.
short_name: Slurry sampling - mixing and location requirements
label: Slurry sampling - mixing and location requirements
fragment_locator: Schedule 2, Part 2, Slurry and other liquid and semi-liquid organic manure, paragraph 2

proposition_text: Dirty water contains 0.5 kg of total nitrogen per cubic metre.
short_name: Nitrogen content in dirty water
label: Nitrogen content in dirty water

proposition_text: In the case of slurry and other liquid and semi-liquid organic manure, at least five samples, each of two litres, must be taken.
short_name: Minimum sampling requirement for slurry and liquid/semi-liquid organic manure
label: Minimum sampling requirement for slurry and liquid/semi-liquid organic manure

proposition_text: The slurry or other manure must, if it is reasonably practicable to do so, be thoroughly mixed before the samples are taken; and each sample must be taken from a different location.
short_name: Mixing and location requirements for slurry sampling
label: Mixing and location requirements for slurry sampling

proposition_text: Each sample must be taken from a vessel; but if a tanker used for spreading is fitted with a suitable valve, the samples may be taken while spreading and each sample must be taken at intervals during the spreading.
short_name: Vessel sampling requirement with tanker exception for slurry
label: Vessel sampling requirement with tanker exception for slurry

proposition_text: Each sample must be poured into a larger container, stirred thoroughly and a two-litre sample taken from that container and poured into a smaller, clean container, and the two-litre sample must then be sent for analysis.
short_name: Sample preparation and submission procedure for slurry
label: Sample preparation and submission procedure for slurry

proposition_text: Solid manure samples must be taken from a manure heap.
short_name: Solid manure sampling location
label: Solid manure sampling location

proposition_text: At least ten samples of 1kg each must be taken from different locations in a manure heap, each at least 0.5 metres from the surface.
short_name: Solid manure sampling quantity and depth
label: Solid manure sampling quantity and depth

proposition_text: Solid manure samples must be placed on a clean, dry tray or sheet, lumps broken up, thoroughly mixed together, and a representative sample of at least 2kg sent for analysis.
short_name: Solid manure sample preparation and submission
label: Solid manure sample preparation and submission

proposition_text: For calculating compliance with the whole farm limit for pigs and poultry, four samples for analysis must be taken in a calendar year (one per quarter) from manure heaps not more than 12 months old.
short_name: Whole farm limit sampling frequency for pigs and poultry
label: Whole farm limit sampling frequency for pigs and poultry

proposition_text: The occupier of a derogated holding must ensure that the area of the holding in hectares is greater than or equal to the total nitrogen in livestock manure from grazing livestock divided by 250 plus the total nitrogen in livestock manure from non-grazing livestock divided by 170, for the calendar year for which the derogation has effect.
short_name: Limit on application of livestock manure nitrogen
label: Limit on application of livestock manure nitrogen

proposition_text: The occupier of a derogated holding must maintain the holding to ensure at least 80% of the agricultural area is sown with grass during the calendar year to which the derogation applies.
short_name: Minimum grassland requirement for derogated holdings
label: Minimum grassland requirement for derogated holdings

proposition_text: Where livestock manure has been spread on grass on sandy soils on a derogated holding between 1st September and 31st December, the grass must not be ploughed before the following 16th January.
short_name: Closed period for ploughing grass on sandy soils after manure spreading
label: Closed period for ploughing grass on sandy soils after manure spreading

proposition_text: In Schedule 3, "the occupier" means the occupier of a derogated holding.
short_name: Definition of "the occupier" in Schedule 3
label: Definition of "the occupier" in Schedule 3

proposition_text: In Schedule 3, "the holding" means the derogated holding.
short_name: Definition of "the holding" in Schedule 3
label: Definition of "the holding" in Schedule 3

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that in the calendar year for which the derogation has effect, the area of the derogated holding (A) is greater than or equal to the sum of (i) the total nitrogen from grazing livestock manure divided by 250, and (ii) the total nitrogen from non-grazing livestock manure divided by 170.
short_name: Derogation area requirement based on nitrogen from livestock manure
label: Derogation area requirement based on nitrogen from livestock manure

proposition_text: For the purposes of the derogation area formula, 'A' means the area of the derogated holding in hectares as at 1st January in the calendar year for which the derogation has effect.
short_name: Definition of 'A' (area) in derogation formula
label: Definition of 'A' (area) in derogation formula

proposition_text: For the purposes of the derogation area formula, 'Ngl' means the total amount of nitrogen in kilogrammes in livestock manure from animals listed as 'grazing livestock' in Schedule 1, whether applied directly by an animal or by spreading.
short_name: Definition of 'Ngl' (nitrogen from grazing livestock) in derogation formula
label: Definition of 'Ngl' (nitrogen from grazing livestock) in derogation formula

proposition_text: For the purposes of the derogation area formula, 'Nngl' means the total amount of nitrogen in kilogrammes in livestock manure from animals listed as 'non-grazing livestock' in Schedule 1, whether applied directly by an animal or by spreading.
short_name: Definition of 'Nngl' (nitrogen from non-grazing livestock) in derogation formula
label: Definition of 'Nngl' (nitrogen from non-grazing livestock) in derogation formula

proposition_text: The occupier must calculate the amount of phosphorus in the soil that is likely to be available for uptake by the crop during the growing season.
short_name: Calculation of available soil phosphorus
label: Calculation of available soil phosphorus

proposition_text: The occupier must calculate the optimum amount of phosphate fertiliser that should be spread on the crop, taking into account the amount of phosphorus available in the soil.
short_name: Calculation of optimum phosphate fertiliser amount
label: Calculation of optimum phosphate fertiliser amount

proposition_text: The occupier must produce a plan for the spreading of phosphate fertiliser for that growing season.
short_name: Production of phosphate fertiliser spreading plan
label: Production of phosphate fertiliser spreading plan

proposition_text: In the case of permanent grassland, the occupier must comply with the phosphate fertiliser planning duties in each calendar year before spreading phosphate fertiliser.
short_name: Timing of compliance for permanent grassland
label: Timing of compliance for permanent grassland

proposition_text: The fertilisation plan for the holding must include a sketch plan indicating the location of the fields to which the plan relates and clearly state the nature of the fertilizer to be used in any field referred to in the plan.
short_name: Fertilisation plan: sketch and fertilizer nature requirements
label: Fertilisation plan: sketch and fertilizer nature requirements

proposition_text: The fertilisation plan must record the amount of phosphorus calculated under paragraph 3(1)(a), the method used to calculate that figure, and the optimum amount of phosphate fertiliser that should be spread on the crop taking into account the calculated phosphorus amount.
short_name: Fertilisation plan: phosphorus calculation and optimum phosphate recording
label: Fertilisation plan: phosphorus calculation and optimum phosphate recording

proposition_text: The fertilisation plan must record the amount of nitrogen likely to be available for uptake by the crop from any organic manure intended to be spread for crop uptake in the growing season during the calendar year in which it is spread, and the amount of phosphate likely to be supplied to meet the requirement of the crop from any organic manure spread or intended to be spread during the calendar year.
short_name: Fertilisation plan: organic manure nitrogen and phosphate availability recording
label: Fertilisation plan: organic manure nitrogen and phosphate availability recording

proposition_text: The fertilisation plan must record the amount of manufactured nitrogen fertiliser required (being the optimum amount of nitrogen required by the crop less the amount of nitrogen that will be available for crop uptake from any organic manure spread during that calendar year) and the amount of manufactured phosphate fertiliser required (being the optimum amount of phosphate required by the crop less the amount of phosphate supplied for crop uptake from any organic manure spread for the purpose of fertilising the crop during that calendar year).
short_name: Fertilisation plan: manufactured fertiliser requirement calculation recording
label: Fertilisation plan: manufactured fertiliser requirement calculation recording

proposition_text: The occupier of a derogated holding must undertake soil sampling and analysis for phosphorus supply at least every four years for at least every five hectares of the agricultural area under the same cropping regime and soil type.
short_name: Phosphorus soil sampling obligation (four-year cycle)
label: Phosphorus soil sampling obligation (four-year cycle)

proposition_text: The occupier may rely on previous phosphorus soil sampling and analysis results if the sampling was carried out within four years before the grant of the derogation.
short_name: Reliance on previous phosphorus sampling
label: Reliance on previous phosphorus sampling

proposition_text: Where phosphorus soil sampling has not been carried out within four years before derogation grant, sampling must be carried out on 75% of the agricultural area no later than 12 months after the grant of the derogation.
short_name: Initial phosphorus sampling requirement (75% within 12 months)
label: Initial phosphorus sampling requirement (75% within 12 months)

proposition_text: Where phosphorus soil sampling has not been carried out within four years before derogation grant, sampling must be carried out on 100% of the agricultural area no later than 12 months after the date on which a derogation is next granted to the occupier.
short_name: Complete phosphorus sampling requirement (100% at next derogation)
label: Complete phosphorus sampling requirement (100% at next derogation)

proposition_text: Before spreading organic manure, the occupier must record the total phosphate content of the organic manure and the amount of phosphate likely to be supplied from the organic manure intended to be spread for the purpose of fertilising the crop in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Recording requirements before spreading organic manure
label: Recording requirements before spreading organic manure

proposition_text: Before spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser, the occupier must record the amount required (that is, the optimum amount of phosphate required by the crop less the amount of phosphate that will be supplied for crop uptake from any organic manure spread).
short_name: Recording phosphate amount required before spreading manufactured fertiliser
label: Recording phosphate amount required before spreading manufactured fertiliser

proposition_text: Before spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser, the occupier must record the month in which it is planned to do the spreading.
short_name: Recording planned spreading month for manufactured phosphate fertiliser
label: Recording planned spreading month for manufactured phosphate fertiliser

proposition_text: The risk map for the holding must show each field marked with a reference number or letter to enable cross-reference to fields recorded in the fertilisation plan.
short_name: Risk map: field reference marking
label: Risk map: field reference marking

proposition_text: The risk map for the holding must correspond with the agricultural area of the holding.
short_name: Risk map: correspondence with agricultural area
label: Risk map: correspondence with agricultural area

proposition_text: The risk map for the holding must be completed by 1st March in the calendar year to which it relates.
short_name: Risk map: completion deadline
label: Risk map: completion deadline

proposition_text: Where a change in circumstances affects a matter referred to in paragraph (1), the occupier must update the risk map within one month of the change.
short_name: Risk map: update obligation on change
label: Risk map: update obligation on change

proposition_text: The occupier must maintain the holding to ensure at least 80% of the agricultural area is sown with grass during the calendar year to which the derogation applies.
short_name: Minimum grass coverage requirement for derogation
label: Minimum grass coverage requirement for derogation

proposition_text: Any temporary grassland on sandy soils on the holding must not be ploughed between 1st July and the following 31st December.
short_name: Prohibition on ploughing temporary grassland on sandy soils (1 July – 31 December)
label: Prohibition on ploughing temporary grassland on sandy soils (1 July – 31 December)

proposition_text: Where livestock manure has been spread on grass on sandy soils on the holding between 1st September and 31st December, the grass must not be ploughed before the following 16th January.
short_name: Prohibition on ploughing grass on sandy soils after manure spreading (until 16 January)
label: Prohibition on ploughing grass on sandy soils after manure spreading (until 16 January)

proposition_text: Where livestock manure has been spread on grass not on sandy soils on the holding between 15th October and the following 15th January, the grass must not be ploughed before the following 16th January.
short_name: Prohibition on ploughing grass not on sandy soils after manure spreading (until 16 January)
label: Prohibition on ploughing grass not on sandy soils after manure spreading (until 16 January)

proposition_text: Where any grass on the holding is ploughed, the land must be sown with a crop with high nitrogen demand within four weeks beginning with the day after the ploughing.
short_name: Ploughed grass land – sowing with high nitrogen demand crop (4 weeks)
label: Ploughed grass land – sowing with high nitrogen demand crop (4 weeks)

proposition_text: A crop with high nitrogen demand includes grass, potatoes, sugar beet, maize, wheat, oilseed rape, barley, brassica, rye and triticale.
short_name: Definition – crop with high nitrogen demand
label: Definition – crop with high nitrogen demand

proposition_text: Crop rotation on the holding must not include leguminous or other plants fixing atmospheric nitrogen, except for grass with less than 50% clover or any other leguminous plant that is under-sown with grass.
short_name: Prohibition on leguminous crops in rotation with exceptions
label: Prohibition on leguminous crops in rotation with exceptions

proposition_text: The occupier must record the total agricultural area and the area of grass within the holding by 1st March for each calendar year.
short_name: Annual recording of agricultural area and grass area by 1st March
label: Annual recording of agricultural area and grass area by 1st March

proposition_text: If the size of the holding or the area of grass within it changes, the occupier must update the record within one month beginning with the day after the day of the change.
short_name: Update record within one month of change in holding or grass area
label: Update record within one month of change in holding or grass area

proposition_text: The occupier must make a record describing the livestock housing and manure storage systems together with the volume of manure storage in place on the holding by 1st March for each calendar year.
short_name: Annual record of livestock housing and manure storage by 1 March
label: Annual record of livestock housing and manure storage by 1 March

proposition_text: The occupier must record the expected number of livestock animals to be kept on the holding during the calendar year to which the derogation relates, and the category for each animal by reference to the categories in Schedule 1.
short_name: Record expected livestock numbers and categories
label: Record expected livestock numbers and categories

proposition_text: Having complied with the duty to record expected livestock numbers, the occupier must calculate and record the amount of nitrogen and phosphate in manure expected to be produced by the livestock on the holding during that year by reference to Schedule 1.
short_name: Calculate and record expected nitrogen and phosphate in manure
label: Calculate and record expected nitrogen and phosphate in manure

proposition_text: A record required under paragraph 14 must be made by 1st March for the calendar year concerned.
short_name: Deadline for making records
label: Deadline for making records

proposition_text: The occupier must make a record of the type and amount of livestock manure that is intended to be brought on to the holding and sent off the holding during the calendar year to which the derogation relates.
short_name: Record livestock manure movements (derogation)
label: Record livestock manure movements (derogation)

proposition_text: The occupier must calculate and record the amount of nitrogen in the livestock manure recorded under paragraph (a) in accordance with Part 1 or 2 of Schedule 2.
short_name: Calculate and record nitrogen in livestock manure (derogation)
label: Calculate and record nitrogen in livestock manure (derogation)

proposition_text: A record required under paragraph 15 must be made by 1st March for the calendar year concerned.
short_name: Deadline for livestock manure records (derogation)
label: Deadline for livestock manure records (derogation)

proposition_text: The occupier must, within one week of sowing a crop on which nitrogen fertiliser is or will be spread, record the crop sown and the date of sowing.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for crops receiving nitrogen fertiliser
label: Record-keeping obligation for crops receiving nitrogen fertiliser

proposition_text: The occupier must record the total phosphorus content within one week of spreading organic manure.
short_name: Record total phosphorus content within one week of spreading organic manure
label: Record total phosphorus content within one week of spreading organic manure

proposition_text: The occupier must record the amount of phosphate that was supplied for uptake by the crop within one week of spreading organic manure.
short_name: Record phosphate supplied for crop uptake within one week of spreading organic manure
label: Record phosphate supplied for crop uptake within one week of spreading organic manure

proposition_text: The occupier must record the date of spreading within one week of spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser.
short_name: Record date of spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser within one week
label: Record date of spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser within one week

proposition_text: The occupier must record the amount of phosphate spread within one week of spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser.
short_name: Record amount of phosphate spread from manufactured fertiliser within one week
label: Record amount of phosphate spread from manufactured fertiliser within one week

proposition_text: The occupier must record the date of ploughing within one week of ploughing on the holding.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for ploughing date
label: Record-keeping obligation for ploughing date

proposition_text: The occupier must prepare accounts for the calendar year to which the derogation relates and submit them to the Agency by 30th April of the following year.
short_name: Derogation accounts: preparation and submission deadline
label: Derogation accounts: preparation and submission deadline

proposition_text: The accounts must be in the form and manner published by the Agency.
short_name: Derogation accounts: form and manner requirement
label: Derogation accounts: form and manner requirement

proposition_text: The accounts must record the total agricultural area of the holding and the area covered by specified crop types including winter wheat, spring wheat, winter barley, spring barley, winter oilseed rape, sugar beet, potatoes, forage maize, grass, and other crops.
short_name: Derogation accounts: agricultural area and crop recording
label: Derogation accounts: agricultural area and crop recording

proposition_text: The accounts must record the number and category of animals kept on the holding, the amount of nitrogen and phosphate in manure produced by those animals, the amount, type and characteristics of livestock manure brought on to or sent off the holding, the nitrogen and phosphate content of that manure, and the weight and nitrogen content of manufactured nitrogen fertiliser stocks and movements between 1st January and 31st December.
short_name: Derogation accounts: livestock, manure and fertiliser recording
label: Derogation accounts: livestock, manure and fertiliser recording

proposition_text: Regulations 7, 8, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16, 17, 19, 23, 25A, 25B, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32, and 34 do not apply to a new holding until the beginning of the second calendar year after the year in which the Secretary of State revises or adds to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones under regulation 4(5) so as to include the new holding.
short_name: Transitional exemption for new holdings in nitrate vulnerable zones
label: Transitional exemption for new holdings in nitrate vulnerable zones

proposition_text: Regulation 18 (restrictions on spreading slurry) does not apply in relation to a new holding until 31st July in the third calendar year after the year in which the Secretary of State revises or adds to the designation of nitrate vulnerable zones under regulation 4(5) so as to include the new holding.
short_name: Transitional exemption from slurry spreading restrictions for new holdings
label: Transitional exemption from slurry spreading restrictions for new holdings

proposition_text: The Water Resources (Control of Agricultural Pollution) (Wales) Regulations 2021 apply in relation to Wales.
short_name: Territorial application
label: Territorial application

proposition_text: The Water Resources (Control of Agricultural Pollution) (Wales) Regulations 2021 come into force on 1 April 2021.
short_name: Commencement date
label: Commencement date

proposition_text: In a holding or part of a holding that was not previously situated within a nitrate vulnerable zone as shown on the NVZ index map 2013, regulations 5 to 11, 15, 23, 27, 33 to 35, and 37 to 43 do not apply until 1 January 2023.
short_name: Transitional non-application of specified regulations until 1 January 2023 for newly designated NVZ holdings
label: Transitional non-application of specified regulations until 1 January 2023 for newly designated NVZ holdings

proposition_text: In a holding or part of a holding that was not previously situated within a nitrate vulnerable zone as shown on the NVZ index map 2013, regulations 4 and 36 do not apply until 1 January 2024, except that regulation 4 does not apply to qualifying grassland holdings until 1 January 2025.
short_name: Transitional non-application of regulations 4 and 36 until 1 January 2024 (or 2025 for qualifying grassland) for newly designated NVZ holdings
label: Transitional non-application of regulations 4 and 36 until 1 January 2024 (or 2025 for qualifying grassland) for newly designated NVZ holdings

proposition_text: In a holding or part of a holding that was not previously situated within a nitrate vulnerable zone as shown on the NVZ index map 2013, regulations 17 to 21, 25, 26, and 28 to 31 do not apply until 1 August 2024.
short_name: Transitional non-application of specified regulations until 1 August 2024 for newly designated NVZ holdings
label: Transitional non-application of specified regulations until 1 August 2024 for newly designated NVZ holdings

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must ensure that in any year beginning 1 January, the total amount of nitrogen in livestock manure applied to the holding (whether directly by an animal or by spreading) does not exceed 170 kg multiplied by the area of the holding in hectares.
short_name: Annual nitrogen limit from livestock manure
label: Annual nitrogen limit from livestock manure

proposition_text: The amount of nitrogen produced by livestock must be calculated in accordance with Schedule 1.
