short_name: Nitrogen calculation methodology
label: Nitrogen calculation methodology

proposition_text: In calculating the area of the holding for the purposes of ascertaining the amount of nitrogen permitted to be spread on the holding, no account is taken of surface waters, any hardstanding, buildings, roads or any woodland unless that woodland is used for grazing.
short_name: Exclusions from holding area calculation
label: Exclusions from holding area calculation

proposition_text: The occupier of a qualifying grassland holding must ensure that during the relevant period the area of the holding (in hectares) is greater than or equal to the total nitrogen in grazing livestock manure divided by 250 plus or minus the total nitrogen in non-grazing livestock manure divided by 170.
short_name: Nitrogen application formula for qualifying grassland holdings
label: Nitrogen application formula for qualifying grassland holdings

proposition_text: Where the occupier of a qualifying grassland holding intends to apply nitrogen in grazing livestock manure exceeding 170 kg multiplied by the area of the holding in hectares, they must submit a notice to NRW in accordance with regulation 4B.
short_name: Notice requirement for exceeding 170 kg nitrogen threshold
label: Notice requirement for exceeding 170 kg nitrogen threshold

proposition_text: In calculating the area of a holding for nitrogen spreading purposes, no account is taken of surface waters, any hardstanding, buildings, roads or any woodland (unless that woodland is used for grazing).
short_name: Exclusions from holding area calculation
label: Exclusions from holding area calculation

proposition_text: A notice under this regulation must include the name of the occupier of the qualifying grassland holding, the address of the qualifying grassland holding, a written statement that the occupier intends during the relevant period to apply to the holding a total amount of nitrogen in grazing livestock manure which exceeds 170 kg multiplied by the area of the holding in hectares, and a written statement that the occupier will comply with the additional requirements set out in Schedule 1A.
short_name: Content requirements for notice under regulation 4B
label: Content requirements for notice under regulation 4B

proposition_text: A notice must be submitted to NRW by 31 March 2024 and must be accompanied by the enhanced nutrient management plan for the qualifying grassland holding.
short_name: Deadline and accompanying document for notice submission
label: Deadline and accompanying document for notice submission

proposition_text: The notice and accompanying enhanced nutrient management plan must be submitted to NRW by e-mail.
short_name: Method of submission for notice and plan
label: Method of submission for notice and plan

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must ensure that, in any 12 month period, the total amount of nitrogen in organic manure spread on any given hectare on the holding does not exceed 250 kg, subject to exceptions for certified compost.
short_name: Annual nitrogen limit for organic manure (250 kg/ha)
label: Annual nitrogen limit for organic manure (250 kg/ha)

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must ensure that the total amount of nitrogen in organic manure exclusively in the form of certified compost applied as mulch to orchard land does not exceed 1000 kg in any four year period.
short_name: Nitrogen limit for certified compost on orchard land (1000 kg/4 years)
label: Nitrogen limit for certified compost on orchard land (1000 kg/4 years)

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must ensure that the total amount of nitrogen in organic manure exclusively in the form of certified compost applied to any land other than orchard land does not exceed 500 kg in any two year period.
short_name: Nitrogen limit for certified compost on other land (500 kg/2 years)
label: Nitrogen limit for certified compost on other land (500 kg/2 years)

proposition_text: Certified compost means green compost or green/food compost in relation to which the supplier confirms in writing that it meets the standards set out in PAS 100:2011 and contains no livestock manure, and the occupier must retain that written confirmation.
short_name: Definition of certified compost and retention requirement
label: Definition of certified compost and retention requirement

proposition_text: Before spreading organic manure, the occupier must on each occasion calculate the amount of nitrogen from that manure that is likely to be available for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Calculation of available nitrogen from organic manure before spreading
label: Calculation of available nitrogen from organic manure before spreading

proposition_text: Before spreading organic manure, the occupier must record the area on which the organic manure will be spread, the quantity of organic manure to be spread, the planned date for spreading (month), the type of organic manure, the total nitrogen content, and the amount of nitrogen likely to be available from the organic manure for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Record-keeping requirements before spreading organic manure
label: Record-keeping requirements before spreading organic manure

proposition_text: Before spreading nitrogen fertiliser, the occupier must record the amount required and the planned date for spreading (month).
short_name: Record-keeping requirements before spreading nitrogen fertiliser
label: Record-keeping requirements before spreading nitrogen fertiliser

proposition_text: An occupier must ensure that the total amount of nitrogen from manufactured nitrogen fertiliser and nitrogen available for crop uptake from organic manure spread in the growing season does not exceed the limits set out in regulation 10 in any 12 month period, irrespective of the figure in the plan.
short_name: Total nitrogen limit (manufactured fertiliser and organic manure)
label: Total nitrogen limit (manufactured fertiliser and organic manure)

proposition_text: The occupier must establish the total amount of nitrogen in livestock manure by using the table in Part 1 of Schedule 3 or by sampling and analysis in accordance with Part 2 of Schedule 3.
short_name: Nitrogen determination in livestock manure
label: Nitrogen determination in livestock manure

proposition_text: For cattle slurry, 40% of the total nitrogen in the livestock manure is assumed to be available for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Cattle slurry nitrogen availability
label: Cattle slurry nitrogen availability

proposition_text: For pig slurry, 50% of the total nitrogen in the livestock manure is assumed to be available for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread.
short_name: Pig slurry nitrogen availability
label: Pig slurry nitrogen availability

proposition_text: For all other organic manure, the occupier must establish the total amount of nitrogen available for crop uptake in the growing season in which it is spread by reference to technical analyses provided by the supplier, or if unavailable, by reference to the Nutrient Management Guide (RB209), or by sampling and analysis in accordance with Part 2 of Schedule 3.
short_name: Nitrogen determination in other organic manure
label: Nitrogen determination in other organic manure

proposition_text: The total amount of nitrogen permitted to be spread on any crop is the figure given in the table, adjusted in accordance with the notes to the table and multiplied by the total area in hectares of that crop sown on the holding.
short_name: Maximum nitrogen spreading limits per crop
label: Maximum nitrogen spreading limits per crop

proposition_text: An additional 80 kg per hectare of nitrogen is permitted to all crops grown in fields if the current or previous crop has had straw or paper sludge applied to it.
short_name: Additional nitrogen for straw/paper sludge application
label: Additional nitrogen for straw/paper sludge application

proposition_text: An additional 20 kg per hectare of nitrogen is permitted on fields with shallow soil (other than shallow soils over sandstone).
short_name: Additional nitrogen for shallow soil
label: Additional nitrogen for shallow soil

proposition_text: An additional 20 kg per hectare of nitrogen is permitted for every tonne that the expected yield exceeds the standard yield.
short_name: Additional nitrogen for yield exceeding standard
label: Additional nitrogen for yield exceeding standard

proposition_text: An occupier who intends to spread nitrogen fertiliser must first undertake a field inspection to consider the risk of nitrogen getting into surface water.
short_name: Field inspection required before spreading nitrogen fertiliser
label: Field inspection required before spreading nitrogen fertiliser

proposition_text: No person may spread nitrogen fertiliser on land if there is a significant risk of nitrogen getting into surface water, taking into account the slope of the land (particularly if more than 12°), ground cover, proximity to surface water, weather conditions, soil type, and the presence of land drains.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading nitrogen fertiliser where significant risk to surface water
label: Prohibition on spreading nitrogen fertiliser where significant risk to surface water

proposition_text: No person may spread nitrogen fertiliser if the soil is waterlogged, flooded, snow covered, frozen, or has been frozen for more than 12 hours in the previous 24 hours.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading nitrogen fertiliser in adverse soil conditions
label: Prohibition on spreading nitrogen fertiliser in adverse soil conditions

proposition_text: No person may spread manufactured nitrogen fertiliser within 2 metres of surface water.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser within 2 metres of surface water
label: Prohibition on spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser within 2 metres of surface water

proposition_text: No person may spread organic manure within 10 metres of surface water unless using precision spreading equipment, in which case no person may spread organic manure within 6 metres of surface water, except where paragraph 26 of Schedule 1A applies.
short_name: Organic manure spreading – minimum distance from surface water
label: Organic manure spreading – minimum distance from surface water

proposition_text: Livestock manure (other than slurry and poultry manure) may be spread within 10 metres of surface water if it is spread on land managed for breeding wader birds or as species-rich semi-natural grassland that is notified as a SSSI or subject to an agri-environment commitment, spread between 1 June and 31 October inclusive, not spread directly on to surface water, and the total annual amount does not exceed 12.5 tonnes per hectare.
short_name: Livestock manure spreading – exception for conservation land
label: Livestock manure spreading – exception for conservation land

proposition_text: No person may spread organic manure within 50 metres of a borehole, spring or well.
short_name: Organic manure spreading – minimum distance from water sources
label: Organic manure spreading – minimum distance from water sources

proposition_text: Any person spreading slurry must use spreading equipment with a low spreading trajectory, that is, below 4 metres from the ground.
short_name: Slurry spreading equipment trajectory requirement
label: Slurry spreading equipment trajectory requirement

proposition_text: Spreading equipment with a spreading trajectory of more than 4 metres from the ground may be used on land that has a low run off risk where such equipment can achieve an average slurry application rate of not more than 2 mm per hour when it is operating continuously.
short_name: Exception for high-trajectory slurry spreading equipment
label: Exception for high-trajectory slurry spreading equipment

proposition_text: Any person spreading nitrogen fertiliser must do so in as accurate a manner as possible.
short_name: Nitrogen fertiliser spreading accuracy requirement
label: Nitrogen fertiliser spreading accuracy requirement

proposition_text: Any person who applies organic manure onto the surface of bare soil or stubble (other than soil that has been sown) must ensure that it is incorporated into the soil in accordance with this regulation.
short_name: Duty to incorporate organic manure applied to bare soil or stubble
label: Duty to incorporate organic manure applied to bare soil or stubble

proposition_text: Poultry manure must be incorporated as soon as practicable, and within 24 hours at the latest.
short_name: Incorporation timing for poultry manure
label: Incorporation timing for poultry manure

proposition_text: Slurry and liquid digested sewage sludge must be incorporated as soon as practicable, and within 24 hours at the latest, unless it was applied using equipment of a type described in regulation 14(4).
short_name: Incorporation timing for slurry and liquid digested sewage sludge
label: Incorporation timing for slurry and liquid digested sewage sludge

proposition_text: Any other organic manure (other than organic manure spread as a mulch on sandy soil) must be incorporated into the soil as soon as practicable, and within 24 hours at the latest, if the land is within 50 metres of surface water and slopes in such a way that there may be run-off to that water.
short_name: Incorporation timing for other organic manure near surface water
label: Incorporation timing for other organic manure near surface water

proposition_text: Organic manure with high readily available nitrogen is defined as organic manure in which more than 30% of the total nitrogen content is available to the crop at the time of spreading.
short_name: Definition of organic manure with high readily available nitrogen
label: Definition of organic manure with high readily available nitrogen

proposition_text: Spreading organic manure with high readily available nitrogen on tillage land with sandy or shallow soil is permitted between 1 August and 15 September inclusive, provided that the crop is sown on or before 15 September.
short_name: Permitted spreading of high readily available nitrogen organic manure on sandy/shallow tillage land (1 Aug–15 Sep) if crop sown by 15 Sep
label: Permitted spreading of high readily available nitrogen organic manure on sandy/shallow tillage land (1 Aug–15 Sep) if crop sown by 15 Sep

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who has submitted an undertaking to the organic control system under Council Regulation (EC) 834/2007 may spread organic manure with high readily available nitrogen at any time on crops listed in Schedule 4 (permitted crops for the closed period), provided that each hectare does not receive more than 150 kg total nitrogen between the start of the closed period and the end of February.
short_name: Organic manure spreading on Schedule 4 crops during closed period
label: Organic manure spreading on Schedule 4 crops during closed period

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who has submitted an undertaking to the organic control system under Council Regulation (EC) 834/2007 may spread organic manure with high readily available nitrogen at any time on other crops (not listed in Schedule 4) in accordance with written advice from a FACTS-qualified person, provided that each hectare does not receive more than 150 kg total nitrogen between the start of the closed period and the end of February.
short_name: Organic manure spreading on other crops with FACTS advice during closed period
label: Organic manure spreading on other crops with FACTS advice during closed period

proposition_text: From the end of the closed period until the end of February, the maximum amount of slurry that may be spread at any one time is 30 cubic metres per hectare.
short_name: Maximum slurry spreading limit (end of closed period to end of February)
label: Maximum slurry spreading limit (end of closed period to end of February)

proposition_text: From the end of the closed period until the end of February, the maximum amount of poultry manure that may be spread at any one time is 8 tonnes per hectare.
short_name: Maximum poultry manure spreading limit (end of closed period to end of February)
label: Maximum poultry manure spreading limit (end of closed period to end of February)

proposition_text: From the end of the closed period until the end of February, there must be at least three weeks between each spreading.
short_name: Minimum interval between spreading events (end of closed period to end of February)
label: Minimum interval between spreading events (end of closed period to end of February)

proposition_text: No person may spread manufactured nitrogen fertiliser on grassland from 15 September to 15 January (inclusive).
short_name: Prohibition on spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser on grassland during closed period
label: Prohibition on spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser on grassland during closed period

proposition_text: Spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser during the closed periods is permitted on crops specified in Schedule 4 Table provided the maximum rate in column 2 is not exceeded.
short_name: Exception permitting spreading on Schedule 4 crops subject to maximum rate
label: Exception permitting spreading on Schedule 4 crops subject to maximum rate

proposition_text: Spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser during the closed periods on crops not in Schedule 4 is permitted on the basis of written advice from a person who is a member of the Fertiliser Advisers Certification and Training Scheme.
short_name: Exception permitting spreading on non-Schedule 4 crops with FACTS advice
label: Exception permitting spreading on non-Schedule 4 crops with FACTS advice

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who stores organic manure (other than slurry) must store it in a vessel, in a covered building, on an impermeable surface, or (for solid manure that can be stacked in a free-standing heap and does not drain liquid) on a temporary field site.
short_name: Storage requirements for organic manure (excluding slurry)
label: Storage requirements for organic manure (excluding slurry)

proposition_text: A person with custody or control of silage being made or stored must keep it in a silo satisfying Schedule 5 requirements, or compress it into bales wrapped in impermeable membranes and stored at least 10 metres from inland freshwaters or coastal waters, or (for field silage on open land) give NRW 14 days' notice and ensure the place is at least 10 metres from inland freshwaters or coastal waters and at least 50 metres from the nearest relevant water abstraction point of any protected water supply source.
short_name: Silage storage requirements
label: Silage storage requirements

proposition_text: A water supply source is a protected water supply source if any relevant water abstraction from it is licensed under Part 2 of the Water Resources Act 1991, or if the person making or storing the silage was aware of the source's location before the silage-making began or (if made elsewhere) before it was stored on the land.
short_name: Protected water supply source definition
label: Protected water supply source definition

proposition_text: A person with custody or control of a silage bale must not open or remove the wrapping of the bale within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters which silage effluent could enter as a result.
short_name: Prohibition on opening silage bales near waters
label: Prohibition on opening silage bales near waters

proposition_text: Relevant water abstraction means the abstraction of water for use for human consumption, or domestic purposes (other than human consumption), or manufacturing food or drink for human consumption.
short_name: Relevant water abstraction definition
label: Relevant water abstraction definition

proposition_text: A person having custody or control of slurry must have a slurry storage system that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 6.
short_name: Slurry storage system requirement
label: Slurry storage system requirement

proposition_text: Slurry must be stored in a slurry storage system that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 6.
short_name: Slurry storage location requirement
label: Slurry storage location requirement

proposition_text: The requirement to have a compliant slurry storage system and to store slurry in that system does not apply to slurry while it is stored temporarily in a tanker that is used for transporting slurry on roads or about a farm.
short_name: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers
label: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers

proposition_text: Regulations 24(1) and 25(1) do not apply to a silo or slurry storage system which, before 1 March 1991, was being used for the purpose of making silage or storing slurry.
short_name: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems in use before 1 March 1991
label: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems in use before 1 March 1991

proposition_text: Regulations 24(1) and 25(1) do not apply to a silo or slurry storage system which was constructed before 1 March 1991 for the purpose of making silage or storing slurry, even if not used before that date.
short_name: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems constructed before 1 March 1991
label: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems constructed before 1 March 1991

proposition_text: Regulations 24(1) and 25(1) do not apply to a silo or slurry storage system where a contract for its construction, substantial enlargement or substantial reconstruction was entered into before 1 March 1991 and the work was completed before 1 September 1991.
short_name: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems contracted before 1 March 1991 and completed before 1 September 1991
label: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems contracted before 1 March 1991 and completed before 1 September 1991

proposition_text: Regulations 24(1) and 25(1) do not apply to a silo or slurry storage system where construction, substantial enlargement or substantial reconstruction work was commenced before 1 March 1991 and the work was completed before 1 September 1991.
short_name: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems with work commenced before 1 March 1991 and completed before 1 September 1991
label: Exemption for silos/slurry storage systems with work commenced before 1 March 1991 and completed before 1 September 1991

proposition_text: A temporary field site must not be in a field liable to flooding or becoming waterlogged, within 50 metres of a borehole, spring or well or within 10 metres of surface water or a land drain (other than a sealed impermeable pipe), located in any single position for more than 12 consecutive months, or located in the same place as an earlier one constructed within the last two years.
short_name: Temporary field site location prohibitions
label: Temporary field site location prohibitions

proposition_text: Solid poultry manure that does not have bedding mixed into it and is stored on a temporary field site must be covered with an impermeable material.
short_name: Solid poultry manure covering requirement
label: Solid poultry manure covering requirement

proposition_text: Topsoil must not be removed from the ground upon which a temporary field site is to be constructed.
short_name: Topsoil removal prohibition
label: Topsoil removal prohibition

proposition_text: A temporary field site must not be located within 30 metres of a watercourse on land identified on the risk map as having an incline of greater than 12°, and the surface area of a temporary field site must be as small as reasonably practicable to minimise the leaching effect of rainfall.
short_name: Watercourse proximity and surface area requirements
label: Watercourse proximity and surface area requirements

proposition_text: Separation of slurry into its solid and liquid fractions must be carried out mechanically or on an impermeable surface where the liquid fraction drains into a suitable receptacle.
short_name: Slurry separation requirements
label: Slurry separation requirements

proposition_text: An occupier of a holding who keeps any of the animals specified in Schedule 1 must provide sufficient storage for all slurry produced on the holding during the storage period, and all poultry manure produced in a yard or building on the holding during the storage period.
short_name: Duty to provide sufficient slurry and poultry manure storage
label: Duty to provide sufficient slurry and poultry manure storage

proposition_text: A slurry store must have the capacity to store, in addition to the manure, any rainfall, washings or other liquid that enters the vessel (either directly or indirectly) during the storage period.
short_name: Slurry store capacity must include rainfall and washings
label: Slurry store capacity must include rainfall and washings

proposition_text: Storage facilities are not necessary for slurry or poultry manure sent off the holding or spread on land that has a low run-off risk (provided that spreading is done in accordance with the restrictions on spreading in these Regulations), but in this case storage facilities for an additional one week's manure must be provided as a contingency measure.
short_name: Exception to storage requirement for manure sent off or spread, with contingency
label: Exception to storage requirement for manure sent off or spread, with contingency

proposition_text: For the purposes of regulation 29, the storage period is (a) the period between 1 October and 1 April (all dates inclusive) for pigs and poultry, and (b) the period between 1 October and 1 March (all dates inclusive) in any other case.
short_name: Definition of 'storage period' for slurry and manure storage
label: Definition of 'storage period' for slurry and manure storage

proposition_text: A person served with a regulation 30 notice may appeal to the Welsh Ministers within 28 days beginning on the day after the date on which the notice is served, or such longer period as the Welsh Ministers allow.
short_name: Right to appeal regulation 30 notice within 28 days
label: Right to appeal regulation 30 notice within 28 days

proposition_text: An appeal under regulation 31 must be made by the appellant serving notice on the Welsh Ministers, and the notice must contain or be accompanied by a statement of the grounds of appeal.
short_name: Appeal procedure requires notice with grounds
label: Appeal procedure requires notice with grounds

proposition_text: On determining an appeal under regulation 31, the Welsh Ministers may direct NRW to withdraw the regulation 30 notice, modify any of its requirements, extend the period for compliance with any requirement, or dismiss the appeal.
short_name: Welsh Ministers' powers on determining appeal
label: Welsh Ministers' powers on determining appeal

proposition_text: The period for compliance with a regulation 30 notice against which an appeal has been made is extended so that it expires on the date on which the Welsh Ministers finally determines the appeal or, if the appeal is withdrawn, the date on which it is withdrawn, subject to any direction under paragraph (5).
short_name: Automatic extension of compliance period during appeal
label: Automatic extension of compliance period during appeal

proposition_text: Regulation 32 applies to any silo or slurry storage system whose construction is to be begun on or after 28 April 2021.
short_name: Application of regulation 32 to new or improved silos and slurry storage systems
label: Application of regulation 32 to new or improved silos and slurry storage systems

proposition_text: A person who proposes to have custody or control of silage or slurry that is to be kept in a new or improved store must give NRW notice specifying the type of silo or storage system and its location, at least 14 days before work constructing the new or improved store is to be begun.
short_name: Duty to notify NRW before constructing new or improved silage or slurry store
label: Duty to notify NRW before constructing new or improved silage or slurry store

proposition_text: For the purposes of regulation 32, 'construction' includes substantial enlargement and reconstruction.
short_name: Definition of 'construction' in regulation 32
label: Definition of 'construction' in regulation 32

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding must maintain a record of the total size of the holding calculated in accordance with regulation 4(3).
short_name: Duty to maintain holding size record
label: Duty to maintain holding size record

proposition_text: If the size of the holding changes, the record must be updated within one month.
short_name: Duty to update holding size record within one month of change
label: Duty to update holding size record within one month of change

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding with livestock must maintain a record of the amount of manure that will be produced by the anticipated number of animals that will be kept in a building or on hardstanding during the storage period, using the figures in Schedule 1.
short_name: Duty to record anticipated manure production
label: Duty to record anticipated manure production

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding with livestock must maintain a record of the amount of storage capacity (slurry vessels and hardstanding) required to enable compliance with regulation 29, taking into account the amount of manure intended to be exported from the holding, the amount of manure intended to be spread on land that has a low run-off risk, and in the case of a slurry vessel the amount of liquid other than slurry likely to enter the vessel.
short_name: Duty to record required storage capacity with adjustments
label: Duty to record required storage capacity with adjustments

proposition_text: An occupier who introduces animals on to a holding for the first time must comply with the record-keeping requirements within one month of the introduction of the animals.
short_name: Time limit for first-time livestock introduction record-keeping
label: Time limit for first-time livestock introduction record-keeping

proposition_text: If the amount of storage capacity changes, the occupier must record the change within one week.
short_name: Duty to record storage capacity changes within one week
label: Duty to record storage capacity changes within one week

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding with livestock must, before 30 April each year, record for the previous storage period the number and category of animals in a building or on a hardstanding during the storage period.
short_name: Annual livestock record-keeping obligation
label: Annual livestock record-keeping obligation

proposition_text: The occupier of a holding with livestock must record the sites used for field heaps and the dates of use.
short_name: Field heap site and date record-keeping obligation
label: Field heap site and date record-keeping obligation

proposition_text: Before the relevant date every year, the occupier must record the number and category of animals on the holding during the previous 12 month period and the number of days each animal spent on the holding.
short_name: Annual animal record obligation
label: Annual animal record obligation

proposition_text: The occupier must calculate the amount of nitrogen in the manure produced by the animals on the holding during that year using the Table in Schedule 1.
short_name: Nitrogen calculation using Schedule 1
label: Nitrogen calculation using Schedule 1

proposition_text: For permanently housed pigs or poultry, the occupier may alternatively use approved software or, for systems producing only solid manure, sampling and analysis in accordance with Part 2 of Schedule 3.
short_name: Alternative nitrogen calculation methods
label: Alternative nitrogen calculation methods

proposition_text: The occupier must make a record of the calculations and how the final figures were arrived at.
short_name: Record of calculation methodology
label: Record of calculation methodology

proposition_text: An occupier who brings livestock manure on to a holding must, within one week, record the type and amount of livestock manure, the date it is brought on to the holding, the nitrogen content, and if known the name and address of the supplier.
short_name: Record-keeping for livestock manure brought onto holding
label: Record-keeping for livestock manure brought onto holding

proposition_text: An occupier who sends livestock manure off a holding must within one week record the type and amount of livestock manure, the date it is sent off the holding, the nitrogen content, the name and address of the recipient, and details of a contingency plan to be used in the event that an agreement for a person to accept the livestock manure fails.
short_name: Record-keeping for livestock manure sent off holding
label: Record-keeping for livestock manure sent off holding

proposition_text: If the nitrogen content of the livestock manure brought on to a holding is not known, the occupier must ascertain it as soon as is reasonably practicable after arrival and record it within one week of ascertaining it.
short_name: Duty to ascertain and record unknown nitrogen content
label: Duty to ascertain and record unknown nitrogen content

proposition_text: All nitrogen content of the livestock manure must be ascertained using either the standard figures in Part 1 of Schedule 3 or by sampling and analysis as set out in Part 2 of that Schedule.
short_name: Methods for ascertaining nitrogen content
label: Methods for ascertaining nitrogen content

proposition_text: Any person using sampling and analysis to determine nitrogen content in organic manure must keep the original laboratory report.
short_name: Duty to retain original laboratory report for nitrogen content analysis in organic manure
label: Duty to retain original laboratory report for nitrogen content analysis in organic manure

proposition_text: An occupier who intends to spread nitrogen fertiliser must record the crop sown within one week of sowing a crop.
short_name: Record crop sown within one week
label: Record crop sown within one week

proposition_text: An occupier who intends to spread nitrogen fertiliser must record the date of sowing within one week of sowing a crop.
short_name: Record date of sowing within one week
label: Record date of sowing within one week

proposition_text: Within one week of spreading organic manure, the occupier must record the area on which organic manure is spread, the quantity of organic manure spread, the date or dates, the methods of spreading, the type of organic manure, the total nitrogen content, and the amount of nitrogen that was available to the crop.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for organic manure spreading
label: Record-keeping obligation for organic manure spreading

proposition_text: Within one week of spreading manufactured nitrogen fertiliser, the occupier must record the date of spreading and the amount of nitrogen spread.
short_name: Record-keeping obligation for manufactured nitrogen fertiliser spreading
label: Record-keeping obligation for manufactured nitrogen fertiliser spreading

proposition_text: The record-keeping obligations in paragraphs (1) and (2) do not apply to the occupier of a holding in any calendar year in which 80% of the agricultural area of the holding is sown with grass and the total nitrogen in organic manure applied is no more than 100 kg per hectare, the total nitrogen in manufactured nitrogen fertiliser applied is no more than 90 kg per hectare, and the occupier does not bring any organic manure onto the holding.
short_name: Exception to record-keeping for grass-dominant holdings with low nitrogen application
label: Exception to record-keeping for grass-dominant holdings with low nitrogen application

proposition_text: An occupier who has used nitrogen fertiliser must record the yield achieved by an arable crop within one week of ascertaining it.
short_name: Duty to record arable crop yield after nitrogen fertiliser use
label: Duty to record arable crop yield after nitrogen fertiliser use

proposition_text: Before 30 April each year an occupier must record how any grassland was managed in the previous calendar year.
short_name: Annual duty to record grassland management
label: Annual duty to record grassland management

proposition_text: An occupier must keep a copy of any advice from a person who is a member of the Fertiliser Advisers Certification and Training Scheme that is relied on for any purpose under these Regulations for five years.
short_name: Retention of FACTS adviser advice for five years
label: Retention of FACTS adviser advice for five years

proposition_text: Any person required to make a record under these Regulations must keep it for five years.
short_name: Record retention period
label: Record retention period

proposition_text: The Welsh Ministers must establish a monitoring programme to assess the effectiveness of the measures imposed by these Regulations as a means of reducing or preventing water pollution from agricultural sources.
short_name: Duty to establish monitoring programme for agricultural water pollution measures
label: Duty to establish monitoring programme for agricultural water pollution measures

proposition_text: At least every four years, the Welsh Ministers must review the effectiveness of the measures imposed by these Regulations as a means of reducing or preventing water pollution from agricultural sources and if necessary revise them.
short_name: Duty to review and revise agricultural water pollution measures every four years
label: Duty to review and revise agricultural water pollution measures every four years

proposition_text: When carrying out a review under paragraph (2), the Welsh Ministers must take into account available scientific and technical data, particularly with reference to respective nitrogen contributions originating from agricultural and other sources.
short_name: Duty to consider scientific and technical data on nitrogen contributions in review
label: Duty to consider scientific and technical data on nitrogen contributions in review

proposition_text: If proposals for an alternative suite of measures for delivering the outcomes in regulation 44(1) are received within 18 months of these Regulations coming into force, the Welsh Ministers must consider whether those measures would deliver the outcomes more effectively than the measures contained in these Regulations.
short_name: Duty to consider alternative measures proposals received within 18 months
label: Duty to consider alternative measures proposals received within 18 months

proposition_text: If the Welsh Ministers are satisfied that proposals submitted under paragraph (1) would be more effective in delivering the outcomes in regulation 44(1), they must publish a statement within two years of these Regulations coming into force, explaining what action will be taken.
short_name: Duty to publish statement if alternative proposals more effective
label: Duty to publish statement if alternative proposals more effective

proposition_text: Any person who contravenes any provision of these Regulations (subject to paragraph (2)) is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction, or on conviction on indictment, to a fine.
short_name: General offence for contravention of Regulations
label: General offence for contravention of Regulations

proposition_text: A person who contravenes regulation 32 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 2 on the standard scale.
short_name: Specific offence for contravention of regulation 32
label: Specific offence for contravention of regulation 32

proposition_text: Where a body corporate is guilty of an offence under these Regulations, and that offence is proved to have been committed with the consent or connivance of, or attributable to neglect on the part of, any director, manager, secretary or similar person (or person purporting to act in such capacity), that person as well as the body corporate is guilty of the offence and liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly, subject to paragraph (4).
short_name: Corporate officer liability for offences
label: Corporate officer liability for offences

proposition_text: Paragraph (3) (corporate officer liability) does not apply to contraventions under regulations 24(1), 24(4), 25(1), 30(5) or 32.
short_name: Exception to corporate officer liability for specified regulations
label: Exception to corporate officer liability for specified regulations

proposition_text: The Water Resources (Control of Agricultural Pollution) (Wales) Regulations 2021 are enforced by Natural Resources Wales.
short_name: Enforcement by NRW
label: Enforcement by NRW

proposition_text: The Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage and Slurry) (Wales) Regulations 2010 are revoked on 1 April 2021 in relation to holdings or parts of holdings situated in a nitrate vulnerable zone as shown on the Nitrate Vulnerable Zones Index Map 2013.
short_name: Revocation of 2010 Regulations for NVZ holdings
label: Revocation of 2010 Regulations for NVZ holdings

proposition_text: Regulations 3 and 9 of the Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage and Slurry) (Wales) Regulations 2010 are revoked on 1 April 2021 in relation to all holdings not situated in nitrate vulnerable zones.
short_name: Partial revocation of 2010 Regulations for non-NVZ holdings (regs 3 & 9)
label: Partial revocation of 2010 Regulations for non-NVZ holdings (regs 3 & 9)

proposition_text: All remaining provisions of the Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage and Slurry) (Wales) Regulations 2010 are revoked on 1 August 2024 in relation to all holdings not situated in nitrate vulnerable zones.
short_name: Full revocation of 2010 Regulations for non-NVZ holdings (remaining provisions)
label: Full revocation of 2010 Regulations for non-NVZ holdings (remaining provisions)

proposition_text: The Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Wales) Regulations 2013, the Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2015, and the Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Wales) (Amendment) Regulations 2019 are revoked.
short_name: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2013–2019
label: Revocation of Nitrate Pollution Prevention Regulations 2013–2019

proposition_text: In the Environmental Permitting (England and Wales) Regulations 2016, Schedule 2, paragraph 17(2)(b), the reference to 'the Nitrate Pollution Prevention (Wales) Regulations 2013' is substituted with 'the Water Resources (Control of Agricultural Pollution) (Wales) Regulations 2021'.
short_name: Amendment to Environmental Permitting Regulations 2016 - substitution of reference
label: Amendment to Environmental Permitting Regulations 2016 - substitution of reference

proposition_text: In the Water Environment (Water Framework Directive) (England and Wales) Regulations 2017, Part 2 of Schedule 2, paragraph 21 is substituted with 'The Water Resources (Control of Agricultural Pollution) (Wales) Regulations 2021'.
short_name: Amendment to Water Environment Regulations 2017 - substitution of paragraph 21
label: Amendment to Water Environment Regulations 2017 - substitution of paragraph 21

proposition_text: In the Water Environment (Water Framework Directive) (England and Wales) Regulations 2017, Part 2 of Schedule 2, paragraph 24 is substituted with 'The Water Resources (Control of Agricultural Pollution) (Wales) Regulations 2021'.
short_name: Amendment to Water Environment Regulations 2017 - substitution of paragraph 24
label: Amendment to Water Environment Regulations 2017 - substitution of paragraph 24

proposition_text: In the Conservation of Habitats and Species (England and Wales) Regulations 2017, regulation 104, paragraph (1)(b) and the 'or' before it are omitted.
short_name: Amendment to Conservation of Habitats and Species Regulations 2017 - omission
label: Amendment to Conservation of Habitats and Species Regulations 2017 - omission

proposition_text: Dairy cows after first calf with an annual milk yield more than 9000 litres produce 64 litres of manure, 315 grams of nitrogen, and 142 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by high-yield dairy cows
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by high-yield dairy cows
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Table 1, Cattle - Dairy cows, After first calf, annual milk yield more than 9000 litres

proposition_text: Sows (including their litters up to a weight of 7 kg per piglet) fed on a diet supplemented with synthetic amino acids produce 10.9 litres of manure, 44 grams of nitrogen, and 37 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by sows with litters on amino acid-supplemented diet
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by sows with litters on amino acid-supplemented diet
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Table 2, Pigs - sows (including their litters up to a weight of 7 kg per piglet) fed on a diet supplemented with synthetic amino acids

proposition_text: Pigs weighing from 66 kg and intended for slaughter that are liquid fed produce 10 litres of manure, 33 grams of nitrogen, and 17.9 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by liquid-fed pigs for slaughter (66 kg and over)
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by liquid-fed pigs for slaughter (66 kg and over)
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Table 2, Pigs - Weight from 66 kg and—Intended for slaughter—liquid fed

proposition_text: Chickens used for production of eggs for human consumption from 17 weeks (not caged) produce 0.12 litres of manure, 1.5 grams of nitrogen, and 1.1 grams of phosphate daily.
short_name: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by non-caged laying hens (17 weeks and over)
label: Daily manure, nitrogen and phosphate production by non-caged laying hens (17 weeks and over)
fragment_locator: Schedule 1, Table 2, Poultry - Chickens used for production of eggs for human consumption—from 17 weeks (not caged)

proposition_text: An occupier of a qualifying grassland holding intending to apply nitrogen in grazing livestock manure exceeding 170 kg per hectare during the relevant period must ensure that the only livestock manure applied to the holding is manure produced by the livestock on that holding.
short_name: Livestock manure source restriction for qualifying grassland holdings
label: Livestock manure source restriction for qualifying grassland holdings

proposition_text: An occupier must undertake soil sampling analysis of at least every five hectares of the agricultural area of the holding with the same cropping regime, nutrient management regime and soil type for the purposes of determining the soil phosphorus index.
short_name: Soil sampling analysis requirement for phosphorus indexing
label: Soil sampling analysis requirement for phosphorus indexing

proposition_text: An occupier must ensure that during the relevant period no person spreads organic manure within 15 metres of surface water unless using precision spreading equipment, in which case no person may spread organic manure within 10 metres of surface water.
short_name: Organic manure spreading buffer zones near surface water
label: Organic manure spreading buffer zones near surface water

proposition_text: If the occupier intends to spread slurry on the holding during the relevant period, precision spreading equipment must be used except where it would not be reasonably practicable to do so.
short_name: Precision spreading equipment requirement for slurry
label: Precision spreading equipment requirement for slurry

proposition_text: Enhanced nutrient management requirements apply to the occupier of a qualifying grassland holding who intends during the relevant period to apply to the holding a total amount of nitrogen in grazing livestock manure which exceeds 170 kg multiplied by the area of the holding in hectares.
short_name: Enhanced nutrient management requirements for nitrogen in grazing livestock manure exceeding 170 kg/ha
label: Enhanced nutrient management requirements for nitrogen in grazing livestock manure exceeding 170 kg/ha

proposition_text: "Soil phosphorus index" means a reference to the index number assigned to the soil in accordance with Table 1 of this Schedule, to indicate the level of phosphorus available from the soil.
short_name: Definition of soil phosphorus index
label: Definition of soil phosphorus index

proposition_text: "Soil sampling analysis" means analysis of a soil sample carried out by a soil testing laboratory to analyse soils for phosphorus.
short_name: Definition of soil sampling analysis
label: Definition of soil sampling analysis

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that the only livestock manure applied to the holding during the relevant period is manure produced by the livestock on the holding.
short_name: Livestock manure source restriction for qualifying grassland holdings
label: Livestock manure source restriction for qualifying grassland holdings

proposition_text: The occupier must undertake soil sampling analysis of at least every five hectares of the agricultural area of the holding with the same cropping regime, nutrient management regime and soil type for the purposes of determining the soil phosphorus index for each area of the holding.
short_name: Soil sampling analysis requirement for phosphorus index determination
label: Soil sampling analysis requirement for phosphorus index determination

proposition_text: An occupier may rely on the results of previous soil sampling analysis of the agricultural area of the holding with the same cropping regime, nutrient management regime and soil type, provided such sampling analysis was carried out within the period beginning with 1 January 2020 and ending with 31 December 2023.
short_name: Reliance on previous soil sampling analysis (2020-2023 period)
label: Reliance on previous soil sampling analysis (2020-2023 period)

proposition_text: Where phosphorus soil sampling analysis of the agricultural area of the holding with the same cropping regime, nutrient management regime and soil type has not been carried out within the period 1 January 2020 to 31 December 2023, such sampling analysis must be undertaken within the period beginning with 1 January 2024 and ending with 30 March 2024.
short_name: Mandatory soil sampling deadline where no prior analysis exists
label: Mandatory soil sampling deadline where no prior analysis exists

proposition_text: The occupier must determine the soil phosphorus index for each area of the holding with the same cropping regime, nutrient management regime and soil type by using the results from soil sampling analysis and the values in Table 1 (Phosphorus index).
short_name: Determination of soil phosphorus index by occupier
label: Determination of soil phosphorus index by occupier

proposition_text: The occupier must calculate the optimum amount of phosphate fertiliser (kg) that should be spread on the crop during the relevant period, taking into account the soil phosphorus index, within the period beginning with 1 January 2024 and ending with 30 March 2024.
short_name: Calculation of optimum phosphate fertiliser amount
label: Calculation of optimum phosphate fertiliser amount

proposition_text: An enhanced nutrient management plan for a holding must include a risk map produced in accordance with paragraph 11(1) indicating the location of the fields to which the plan relates, and must clearly state the type of fertiliser to be used in relation to any field referred to in the plan.
short_name: Enhanced nutrient management plan: risk map and fertiliser type requirements
label: Enhanced nutrient management plan: risk map and fertiliser type requirements

proposition_text: An enhanced nutrient management plan must record the soil phosphorus index for each area of the holding with the same cropping regime, nutrient management regime and soil type.
short_name: Enhanced nutrient management plan: soil phosphorus index recording
label: Enhanced nutrient management plan: soil phosphorus index recording

proposition_text: An enhanced nutrient management plan must record the optimum amount of phosphate fertiliser (kg) that should be spread on the crop, taking into account the soil phosphorus index.
short_name: Enhanced nutrient management plan: optimum phosphate fertiliser recording
label: Enhanced nutrient management plan: optimum phosphate fertiliser recording

proposition_text: An enhanced nutrient management plan must record the amount of nitrogen (kg) likely to be available for uptake by the crop from any organic manure intended to be spread for crop uptake in the growing season during the relevant period.
short_name: Enhanced nutrient management plan: nitrogen from organic manure recording
label: Enhanced nutrient management plan: nitrogen from organic manure recording

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that the total amount of phosphate from manufactured phosphate fertiliser and phosphate from organic manure spread in the growing season does not exceed the limits set out in paragraph 9 during the relevant period, irrespective of the figure recorded in the enhanced nutrient management plan.
short_name: Total phosphate application limit (manufactured and organic)
label: Total phosphate application limit (manufactured and organic)

proposition_text: Subject to sub-paragraph (2), the total amount of phosphate spread on any crop listed in the tables must not exceed the figure under the applicable soil phosphorus index number in the same table.
short_name: Maximum phosphate application limits by crop and soil phosphorus index
label: Maximum phosphate application limits by crop and soil phosphorus index

proposition_text: At P Index 4 and 5, up to 60 kg P₂O₅/ha can be used as a starter fertiliser close to the seed, provided the amount applied as a starter dose together with the amount added in the base dressing does not exceed the amount of phosphate required to replace that removed by the previous crop.
short_name: Starter fertiliser exception for P Index 4 and 5
label: Starter fertiliser exception for P Index 4 and 5

proposition_text: Spreading phosphate on grass and other crops above the values stipulated within the tables is permitted subject to prior receipt of written advice from a person who is a member of the Fertiliser Advisers Certification and Training Scheme.
short_name: Exception for exceeding table limits with FACTS advice
label: Exception for exceeding table limits with FACTS advice

proposition_text: Before spreading organic manure during the relevant period, the occupier must record the total phosphate content (kg) of the organic manure.
short_name: Recording phosphate content of organic manure before spreading
label: Recording phosphate content of organic manure before spreading

proposition_text: Before spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser during the relevant period, the occupier must record the amount of phosphate (kg) required, being the optimum amount required by the crop less the amount supplied from any organic manure spread.
short_name: Recording required phosphate amount for manufactured fertiliser
label: Recording required phosphate amount for manufactured fertiliser

proposition_text: Before spreading manufactured phosphate fertiliser during the relevant period, the occupier must record the planned month for spreading.
short_name: Recording planned month for manufactured phosphate fertiliser spreading
label: Recording planned month for manufactured phosphate fertiliser spreading

proposition_text: In addition to the requirements under regulation 11 (risk maps), the risk map must show each field marked with a reference number or number to enable cross reference to fields recorded in fertilisation plans.
short_name: Risk map must show fields with reference numbers for cross-reference to fertilisation plans
label: Risk map must show fields with reference numbers for cross-reference to fertilisation plans

proposition_text: The risk map must correspond with the agricultural area of the holding.
short_name: Risk map must correspond with agricultural area of holding
label: Risk map must correspond with agricultural area of holding

proposition_text: The risk map must be completed by 31 March 2024.
short_name: Risk map completion deadline: 31 March 2024
label: Risk map completion deadline: 31 March 2024

proposition_text: Where a change in circumstances affects a matter referred to in paragraph (1)(a) or (b), the occupier must update the map within one month of the change, beginning with the day after the change.
short_name: Occupier must update risk map within one month of relevant change
label: Occupier must update risk map within one month of relevant change

proposition_text: The occupier must maintain the holding to ensure at least 80% of the agricultural area is sown with grass during the relevant period.
short_name: Minimum grass cover requirement for agricultural holdings
label: Minimum grass cover requirement for agricultural holdings

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that no person ploughs temporary grassland on sandy soils on the holding within the period beginning with 1 July 2024 and ending with 31 December 2024.
short_name: Prohibition on ploughing temporary grassland on sandy soils (1 July–31 December 2024)
label: Prohibition on ploughing temporary grassland on sandy soils (1 July–31 December 2024)

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that no person ploughs grass on sandy soils before 16 January 2024 on the holding where livestock manure has been spread on that grass within the period beginning with 1 September and ending with 31 December in the previous calendar year.
short_name: Prohibition on ploughing manured grass on sandy soils before 16 January 2024
label: Prohibition on ploughing manured grass on sandy soils before 16 January 2024

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that no person ploughs grass on soils that are not sandy soils on the holding before 16 January 2024 where livestock manure has been spread on that grass within the period beginning with 15 October in the previous calendar year and ending with 15 January 2024.
short_name: Prohibition on ploughing manured grass on non-sandy soils before 16 January 2024
label: Prohibition on ploughing manured grass on non-sandy soils before 16 January 2024

proposition_text: Where grass on a holding is ploughed during the relevant period, the land must be sown with a crop with high nitrogen demand within four weeks beginning with the day after the date of ploughing grass.
short_name: Ploughed grass land – sowing with high nitrogen demand crop
label: Ploughed grass land – sowing with high nitrogen demand crop

proposition_text: Crop rotation on the holding during the relevant period must not include leguminous or other plants fixing atmospheric nitrogen, except for grass with less than 50% clover or any other leguminous plants that are under-sown with grass.
short_name: Prohibition on leguminous crops in rotation with exceptions
label: Prohibition on leguminous crops in rotation with exceptions

proposition_text: The occupier must record the total agricultural area and the area of grass within the holding by 1 March 2024.
short_name: Duty to record agricultural area and grass area by 1 March 2024
label: Duty to record agricultural area and grass area by 1 March 2024

proposition_text: If the size of the holding or area of grass within it changes, the occupier must update the record within one month beginning with the day after the change.
short_name: Duty to update record within one month of change in holding or grass area
label: Duty to update record within one month of change in holding or grass area

proposition_text: The occupier must record the expected number and category of livestock to be kept on the holding during the relevant period, in accordance with the categories in Tables 1 and 2 in Schedule 1.
short_name: Record expected livestock numbers and categories
label: Record expected livestock numbers and categories

proposition_text: Following the record of expected livestock numbers, the occupier must calculate and record the amount of nitrogen and phosphate (kg) in manure expected to be produced by the livestock on the holding during the relevant period using Tables 1 and 2 in Schedule 1.
short_name: Calculate and record expected nitrogen and phosphate in manure
label: Calculate and record expected nitrogen and phosphate in manure

proposition_text: The records required under sub-paragraphs (1) and (2) must be made before 1 March 2024.
short_name: Deadline for livestock and manure nutrient records
label: Deadline for livestock and manure nutrient records

proposition_text: The occupier must make a record of the type and amount of livestock manure (tonnes or cubic metres as applicable) that is intended to be sent off the holding during the relevant period.
short_name: Record type and amount of livestock manure intended to be sent off holding
label: Record type and amount of livestock manure intended to be sent off holding

proposition_text: The occupier must assess and record the amount of nitrogen (kg) in the livestock manure recorded under paragraph (a) in accordance with regulation 36(4) and Parts 1 and 2 of Schedule 3.
short_name: Assess and record nitrogen content in livestock manure to be sent off holding
label: Assess and record nitrogen content in livestock manure to be sent off holding

proposition_text: The records to be made under sub-paragraph (1) must be made by 1 March 2024.
short_name: Deadline for livestock manure records
label: Deadline for livestock manure records

proposition_text: If the occupier intends to spread phosphate fertiliser during the relevant period, the occupier must within one week of sowing a crop record the crop sown and the date of sowing, in addition to the requirements of regulation 39.
short_name: Record-keeping for phosphate fertiliser spreading: crop and sowing date
label: Record-keeping for phosphate fertiliser spreading: crop and sowing date

proposition_text: The occupier must, during the relevant period, record within one week of spreading organic manure the total phosphorus content (kg).
short_name: Record phosphorus content of organic manure within one week
label: Record phosphorus content of organic manure within one week

proposition_text: The recording requirements in paragraph 20 are in addition to the requirements of regulation 40 (records of spreading nitrogen fertiliser).
short_name: Paragraph 20 requirements are additional to regulation 40
label: Paragraph 20 requirements are additional to regulation 40

proposition_text: The occupier must record the date of ploughing grass on the holding within one week of that ploughing during the relevant period, in addition to the requirements of regulation 41 (subsequent records).
short_name: Record date of ploughing grass within one week
label: Record date of ploughing grass within one week

proposition_text: The occupier, or any person on behalf of the occupier, must submit fertilisation accounts for the relevant period to NRW by 31 March 2025 by e-mail.
short_name: Duty to submit fertilisation accounts to NRW by 31 March 2025
label: Duty to submit fertilisation accounts to NRW by 31 March 2025

proposition_text: The fertilisation account must record the total agricultural area of the holding in hectares and the area of the holding in hectares covered by specified crop types including winter wheat, spring wheat, winter barley, spring barley, winter oilseed rape, sugar beet, potatoes, forage maize, grass, and other crops.
short_name: Fertilisation account must record agricultural area and crop coverage
label: Fertilisation account must record agricultural area and crop coverage

proposition_text: The fertilisation account must record the number and category of animals kept on the holding during the relevant period in accordance with the categories described in Tables 1 and 2 in Schedule 1, and the amount of nitrogen and phosphate (kg) in the manure produced by those animals calculated using Tables 1 and 2 in Schedule 1.
short_name: Fertilisation account must record livestock numbers and manure nutrient content
label: Fertilisation account must record livestock numbers and manure nutrient content

proposition_text: The fertilisation account must record the weight (tonnes) and nitrogen content (kg) of all manufactured nitrogen fertiliser stocks kept on the holding, brought on to and sent off the holding during the relevant period, and the weight (tonnes) and phosphate content (kg) of all manufactured phosphate fertiliser stocks kept on the holding, brought on to and sent off the holding during the relevant period.
short_name: Fertilisation account must record manufactured fertiliser stocks and movements
label: Fertilisation account must record manufactured fertiliser stocks and movements

proposition_text: The occupier must protect all soil by ensuring that all land is covered by crops, stubbles, residues or other vegetation at all times, except where establishing such cover would create a significant risk of soil erosion and significant risk of nitrogen and phosphorus getting into surface water.
short_name: Soil protection through continuous vegetation cover
label: Soil protection through continuous vegetation cover

proposition_text: Where land has been harvested using a combine harvester, forage harvester or mower, the occupier must ensure that the stubble of the harvested crop remains in the land at all times throughout the relevant period beginning with the first day after harvest and ending with 31 December 2024.
short_name: Stubble retention after harvest (option a)
label: Stubble retention after harvest (option a)

proposition_text: Where land has been harvested using a combine harvester, forage harvester or mower, the occupier must ensure that the land is prepared as a seedbed for a crop or temporary cover crop within 14 days of harvest, beginning with the first day after harvest, and the crop or temporary cover crop is sown within a period of 10 days beginning with the day after final seedbed preparation.
short_name: Seedbed preparation and sowing timescales (option b(i))
label: Seedbed preparation and sowing timescales (option b(i))

proposition_text: Where sowing within the 10-day period would lead to significant risk of soil erosion and nitrogen or phosphorus entering a surface water, the crop or temporary cover crop must be sown as soon as is practicable after the land ceases to be waterlogged.
short_name: Deferred sowing when erosion risk exists (option b(ii))
label: Deferred sowing when erosion risk exists (option b(ii))

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that sites where supplementary feeding for livestock are provided are not located within 20 metres of a watercourse on any land during the relevant period.
short_name: Livestock supplementary feeding sites: 20-metre watercourse buffer
label: Livestock supplementary feeding sites: 20-metre watercourse buffer

proposition_text: The occupier must ensure that sites where supplementary drinking for livestock are provided are not located within 10 metres of a watercourse on any land during the relevant period.
short_name: Livestock supplementary drinking sites: 10-metre watercourse buffer
label: Livestock supplementary drinking sites: 10-metre watercourse buffer

proposition_text: An occupier who intends to spread slurry on the holding during the relevant period must use precision spreading equipment.
short_name: Mandatory use of precision spreading equipment for slurry spreading
label: Mandatory use of precision spreading equipment for slurry spreading

proposition_text: During the relevant period, no person may spread organic manure within 15 metres of surface water unless using precision spreading equipment.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 15 metres of surface water (general)
label: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 15 metres of surface water (general)

proposition_text: When using precision spreading equipment during the relevant period, no person may spread organic manure within 10 metres of surface water.
short_name: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 10 metres of surface water (precision equipment)
label: Prohibition on spreading organic manure within 10 metres of surface water (precision equipment)

proposition_text: Slurry and other liquid and semi-liquid organic manure must be sampled by taking at least five samples, each of 2 litres.
short_name: Minimum sample number and volume for liquid organic manure
label: Minimum sample number and volume for liquid organic manure

proposition_text: If a tanker used for spreading is fitted with a suitable valve, slurry samples may be taken while spreading, and each sample must be taken at intervals during the spreading.
short_name: Alternative tanker sampling method for slurry
label: Alternative tanker sampling method for slurry

proposition_text: In relation to slurry and other liquid and semi-liquid organic manure, at least five samples, each of 2 litres, must be taken.
short_name: Minimum sampling requirement for slurry and liquid/semi-liquid organic manure
label: Minimum sampling requirement for slurry and liquid/semi-liquid organic manure

proposition_text: Subject to sub-paragraph (3), the five samples must be taken from a vessel, with the slurry thoroughly mixed before sampling if reasonably practicable, and each sample must be taken from a different location.
short_name: Vessel sampling procedure for slurry samples
label: Vessel sampling procedure for slurry samples

proposition_text: If a tanker used for spreading is fitted with a suitable valve, the samples may be taken while spreading, and each sample must be taken at intervals during the spreading.
short_name: Alternative tanker sampling procedure during spreading
label: Alternative tanker sampling procedure during spreading

proposition_text: The five samples must be poured into a larger container, stirred thoroughly, and a 2 litre sample must be taken from that container and poured into a smaller clean container, then sent for analysis.
short_name: Composite sample preparation and submission for analysis
label: Composite sample preparation and submission for analysis

proposition_text: Samples of solid manures must be taken from a manure heap.
short_name: Solid manure sampling location
label: Solid manure sampling location

proposition_text: At least ten samples of 1 kg each must be taken from solid manure, each from a different location in a heap, with each sub-sample taken at least 0.5 metres from the surface of the heap.
short_name: Solid manure sampling quantity and depth
label: Solid manure sampling quantity and depth

proposition_text: When calculating compliance with the whole farm limit for pigs and poultry, four samples for analysis must be taken in a calendar year (one in each quarter) from manure heaps not more than 12 months old.
short_name: Quarterly sampling for pig and poultry whole farm limit compliance
label: Quarterly sampling for pig and poultry whole farm limit compliance

proposition_text: Sub-samples of solid manure must be placed on a clean, dry tray or sheet, any lumps broken up and thoroughly mixed together, and a representative sample of at least 2 kg sent for analysis.
short_name: Solid manure sample preparation and submission
label: Solid manure sample preparation and submission

proposition_text: Nitrogen must not be spread on permitted crops after 31 October.
short_name: Prohibition on nitrogen spreading after 31 October
label: Prohibition on nitrogen spreading after 31 October

proposition_text: An additional 50 kg of nitrogen per hectare may be spread every four weeks during the closed period up to the end of harvest.
short_name: Additional nitrogen spreading allowance during closed period
label: Additional nitrogen spreading allowance during closed period

proposition_text: A maximum of 40 kg of nitrogen per hectare may be spread at any one time.
short_name: Maximum single application rate for nitrogen
label: Maximum single application rate for nitrogen

proposition_text: Oilseed rape (winter) has a maximum nitrogen rate of 30 kg per hectare during the closed period.
short_name: Nitrogen limit for winter oilseed rape
label: Nitrogen limit for winter oilseed rape
fragment_locator: Schedule 4, crop table row: Oilseed rape, winter

proposition_text: The base and walls of the silo, its effluent tank and channels and the walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for silo infrastructure
label: Impermeability requirement for silo infrastructure

proposition_text: No part of the silo, its effluent tank or channels or any pipes may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters into which silage effluent could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum separation distance from waterbodies
label: Minimum separation distance from waterbodies

proposition_text: A silo must comply with the provisions of Schedule 5 in order to satisfy the requirement.
short_name: Silo compliance requirement
label: Silo compliance requirement

proposition_text: The base of a silo must extend beyond any walls of the silo.
short_name: Silo base extension requirement
label: Silo base extension requirement

proposition_text: The base of a silo must be provided at its perimeter with channels designed and constructed so as to collect any silage effluent that escapes from the silo.
short_name: Silo perimeter channel requirement
label: Silo perimeter channel requirement

proposition_text: The base of a silo must have adequate provision for the drainage of silage effluent from perimeter channels to an effluent tank through a channel or pipe.
short_name: Silo effluent drainage provision requirement
label: Silo effluent drainage provision requirement

proposition_text: The capacity of the effluent tank must not be less than 20 litres for each cubic metre of silo capacity where the silo has a capacity of less than 1,500 cubic metres.
short_name: Effluent tank minimum capacity for silos < 1,500 m³
label: Effluent tank minimum capacity for silos < 1,500 m³

proposition_text: The capacity of the effluent tank must not be less than 30 cubic metres plus 6.7 litres for each cubic metre of silo capacity in excess of 1,500 cubic metres where the silo has a capacity of 1,500 cubic metres or more.
short_name: Effluent tank minimum capacity for silos ≥ 1,500 m³
label: Effluent tank minimum capacity for silos ≥ 1,500 m³

proposition_text: The base of a silo must be designed in accordance with BS 8007:1987 (code of practice for design of concrete structures for retaining aqueous liquids) or constructed using appropriate hot-rolled asphalt in accordance with BS 5502: Part 21:1990 (code of practice for selection and use of construction materials).
short_name: Silo base design and construction standards
label: Silo base design and construction standards

proposition_text: The base of the silo, the base and walls of its effluent tank and channels, and walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for silo and effluent infrastructure
label: Impermeability requirement for silo and effluent infrastructure

proposition_text: The base and walls of a silo, its effluent tank and channels, and the walls of any pipes must, so far as reasonably practicable, be resistant to attack by silage effluent.
short_name: Silage infrastructure must be resistant to silage effluent attack
label: Silage infrastructure must be resistant to silage effluent attack

proposition_text: No part of a silo, its effluent tank, channels, or pipes may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters into which silage effluent could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Silo and effluent infrastructure: 10-metre separation from waters
label: Silo and effluent infrastructure: 10-metre separation from waters

proposition_text: Retaining walls of a silo must be capable of withstanding minimum wall loadings calculated on the assumptions and in the manner indicated by paragraph 15.6 of BS 5502: Part 22: 2003.
short_name: Silo retaining wall loading capacity requirement
label: Silo retaining wall loading capacity requirement

proposition_text: A silo with retaining walls must at no time be loaded to a depth exceeding the maximum depth consistent with the design assumption made in respect of the loadings of the retaining walls.
short_name: Silo maximum loading depth restriction
label: Silo maximum loading depth restriction

proposition_text: Notices must be displayed on the retaining walls of a silo in accordance with paragraph 18 of BS 5502: Part 22: 2003.
short_name: Silo retaining wall notice display requirement
label: Silo retaining wall notice display requirement

proposition_text: A silo, its effluent tank and channels and any pipes must be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to continue to satisfy the requirements of paragraphs 2 to 5 and, if applicable, paragraph 7(a) for at least 20 years.
short_name: Design and construction durability requirement for silos and effluent infrastructure
label: Design and construction durability requirement for silos and effluent infrastructure

proposition_text: An effluent tank with any part below ground level must be designed and constructed to satisfy structural and impermeability requirements for at least 20 years without maintenance.
short_name: Below-ground effluent tank: 20-year design life without maintenance
label: Below-ground effluent tank: 20-year design life without maintenance

proposition_text: The base of a slurry storage tank, the base and walls of any effluent tank, channels and reception pit, and the walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage system components
label: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage system components

proposition_text: The base and walls of the slurry storage tank, any effluent tank, channels and reception pit, and the walls of any pipes must be protected against corrosion in accordance with paragraph 7 of BS 5502: Part 50: 1993.
short_name: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry storage system components
label: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry storage system components

proposition_text: No part of the slurry storage tank or any effluent tank, channels or reception pit may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters into which slurry could enter if it were to escape, unless precautions are taken that NRW agrees in writing are adequate to avoid any significant risk of pollution of controlled waters.
short_name: Minimum distance requirement from waters for slurry storage systems
label: Minimum distance requirement from waters for slurry storage systems

proposition_text: A slurry storage system must satisfy specified requirements.
short_name: Slurry storage system requirements
label: Slurry storage system requirements

proposition_text: The base of a slurry storage tank must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement: slurry storage tank base
label: Impermeability requirement: slurry storage tank base

proposition_text: The base and walls of any effluent tank, channels and reception pit must be impermeable.
