short_name: Impermeability requirement: effluent tank, channels, reception pit
label: Impermeability requirement: effluent tank, channels, reception pit
fragment_locator: schedule 6, paragraph 2 (effluent tank, channels, reception pit)

proposition_text: The walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement: pipe walls
label: Impermeability requirement: pipe walls

proposition_text: The base and walls of slurry storage tanks, effluent tanks, channels, reception pits, and the walls of pipes must be protected against corrosion in accordance with BS 5502: Part 50: 1993, paragraph 7.
short_name: Corrosion protection for slurry and effluent storage infrastructure
label: Corrosion protection for slurry and effluent storage infrastructure

proposition_text: The base and walls of a slurry storage tank and of any reception pit must be capable of withstanding characteristic loads calculated on the assumptions and in the manner indicated by paragraph 5 of BS 5502: Part 50: 1993.
short_name: Structural load-bearing requirements for slurry storage tanks and reception pits
label: Structural load-bearing requirements for slurry storage tanks and reception pits

proposition_text: Facilities used for the temporary storage of slurry before transfer to a slurry storage tank must have adequate capacity to store the maximum quantity of slurry likely to be produced on the premises in any two day period (disregarding slurry transferred directly into a slurry storage tank).
short_name: Temporary slurry storage capacity requirement (two-day maximum)
label: Temporary slurry storage capacity requirement (two-day maximum)

proposition_text: Facilities used for the temporary storage of slurry before transfer to a slurry storage tank may have a lesser capacity than the two-day maximum if NRW agrees in writing that the lesser capacity is adequate to avoid any significant risk of pollution of controlled waters.
short_name: Temporary slurry storage capacity exception (NRW agreement)
label: Temporary slurry storage capacity exception (NRW agreement)

proposition_text: Where slurry flows into a channel before discharging into a reception pit and the flow of slurry out of the channel is controlled by means of a sluice, the capacity of the reception pit must be adequate to hold the maximum quantity of slurry that can be released by opening the sluice.
short_name: Reception pit capacity requirement (sluice-controlled channel)
label: Reception pit capacity requirement (sluice-controlled channel)

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank with walls made of earth must have at least 750 mm of freeboard.
short_name: Freeboard requirement for earth-walled slurry storage tanks
label: Freeboard requirement for earth-walled slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank (other than one with walls made of earth) must have at least 300 mm of freeboard.
short_name: Freeboard requirement for non-earth-walled slurry storage tanks
label: Freeboard requirement for non-earth-walled slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: No part of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, channels, or reception pit may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters into which slurry could enter if it were to escape, unless precautions agreed in writing by NRW are taken that are adequate to avoid any significant risk of pollution of controlled waters.
short_name: 10-metre separation distance for slurry storage from waters (or NRW-agreed precautions)
label: 10-metre separation distance for slurry storage from waters (or NRW-agreed precautions)

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank and any effluent tank, channels, pipes and reception pit must be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to continue to satisfy the requirements of paragraphs 2 to 4 for at least 20 years.
short_name: Design and construction durability requirement for slurry storage and effluent infrastructure
label: Design and construction durability requirement for slurry storage and effluent infrastructure

proposition_text: If the walls of a slurry storage tank are not impermeable, the base of the tank must extend beyond the walls.
short_name: Base extension requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank walls
label: Base extension requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank walls

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank, effluent tank, or reception pit fitted with a drainage pipe must have two valves in series on the pipe, each valve separated from the other by a minimum distance of 1 metre, subject to exceptions.
short_name: Two-valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry/effluent tanks
label: Two-valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry/effluent tanks

proposition_text: Each valve on a drainage pipe of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, or reception pit must be capable of shutting off the flow of slurry through the pipe and must be kept shut and locked in that position when not in use.
short_name: Valve capability and operational requirements
label: Valve capability and operational requirements

proposition_text: The two-valve requirement does not apply to a slurry storage tank that drains through the pipe into another slurry storage tank if the other tank is of equal or greater capacity.
short_name: Exception: equal or greater capacity receiving tank
label: Exception: equal or greater capacity receiving tank

proposition_text: The two-valve requirement does not apply to a slurry storage tank that drains through the pipe into another slurry storage tank if the tops of the tanks are at the same level.
short_name: Exception: same-level tank tops
label: Exception: same-level tank tops

proposition_text: These Regulations may be cited as the Water Resources (Control of Pollution) (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (England) Regulations 2010.
short_name: Citation
label: Citation

proposition_text: These Regulations apply to England only.
short_name: Territorial extent
label: Territorial extent

proposition_text: These Regulations come into force on 6th April 2010.
short_name: Commencement
label: Commencement

proposition_text: Slurry is defined as liquid or semi-liquid matter composed of excreta produced by livestock while in a yard or building (including that held in wood chip corrals), or a mixture wholly or mainly consisting of livestock excreta, livestock bedding, rainwater and washings from a building or yard used by livestock, of a consistency that allows it to be pumped or discharged by gravity at any stage in the handling process.
short_name: Definition of slurry
label: Definition of slurry

proposition_text: A slurry storage system includes a slurry storage tank and any reception pit and any effluent tank used in connection with the tank, and any channels and pipes used in connection with the tank, any reception pit or any effluent tank.
short_name: Components of slurry storage system
label: Components of slurry storage system

proposition_text: Silage effluent means effluent from silage.
short_name: Definition of silage effluent
label: Definition of silage effluent

proposition_text: A requirement for a silo or slurry storage tank to conform to a British Standard is satisfied if the silo or tank conforms to a standard or specification that provides an equivalent level of protection and performance.
short_name: Equivalence to British Standard for slurry storage tanks
label: Equivalence to British Standard for slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: A person with custody or control of silage being made or stored must keep it in a silo satisfying Schedule 1 requirements, or compress it into bales wrapped in impermeable membranes and stored at least 10 metres from inland freshwaters or coastal waters, or (for field silage on open land) give the Agency 14 days' notice and ensure the place is at least 10 metres from inland freshwaters or coastal waters and at least 50 metres from the nearest relevant abstraction point of any protected water supply source.
short_name: Silage storage requirements: silo, baled, or field silage conditions
label: Silage storage requirements: silo, baled, or field silage conditions

proposition_text: A water supply source is a protected water supply source if any relevant water abstraction from the source is licensed under Part II of the Water Resources Act 1991, or the person making or storing the silage was aware of the source's location before the making of the silage began or (if the silage was made elsewhere) before it was stored on the land in question.
short_name: Definition of protected water supply source for silage storage
label: Definition of protected water supply source for silage storage

proposition_text: A person who has custody or control of a silage bale must not open or remove the wrapping of the bale within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters which silage effluent could enter as a result.
short_name: Prohibition on opening silage bale wrapping near waters
label: Prohibition on opening silage bale wrapping near waters

proposition_text: For the purposes of silage storage requirements, 'relevant water abstraction' means the abstraction of water for use for human consumption, or domestic purposes (other than human consumption), or manufacturing food or drink for human consumption; and 'water supply source' means inland freshwaters or ground waters from which a relevant abstraction is made or licensed to be made.
short_name: Definitions of relevant water abstraction and water supply source
label: Definitions of relevant water abstraction and water supply source

proposition_text: A person having custody or control of slurry must store it only in a slurry storage system that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 2.
short_name: Slurry storage system requirement
label: Slurry storage system requirement

proposition_text: The requirement to store slurry in a compliant slurry storage system does not apply to slurry while it is stored temporarily in a tanker that is used for transporting slurry on roads or about a farm.
short_name: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers
label: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers

proposition_text: A person who has custody or control of fuel oil on a farm must ensure that it is stored in a fuel storage tank within a storage area or facility that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 3, in drums within such a storage area, or in an underground fuel storage tank, subject to exceptions.
short_name: Fuel oil storage requirements on farms
label: Fuel oil storage requirements on farms

proposition_text: The fuel oil storage requirements do not apply to the temporary storage of fuel oil in a tanker used for transporting fuel oil on roads or about a farm.
short_name: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers
label: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers

proposition_text: The fuel oil storage requirements do not apply where the total quantity of fuel oil stored on the farm does not exceed 1,500 litres.
short_name: Exception for small quantities under 1,500 litres
label: Exception for small quantities under 1,500 litres

proposition_text: These Regulations do not apply to a silo, slurry storage system or fuel storage tank which, before 1st March 1991, was being used for the purpose of making silage, storing slurry or storing fuel oil.
short_name: Exemption for pre-1991 operational slurry storage systems
label: Exemption for pre-1991 operational slurry storage systems

proposition_text: These Regulations do not apply to a silo, slurry storage system or fuel storage tank which was constructed before 1st March 1991 for the purpose of making silage, storing slurry or storing fuel oil, even if not used before that date.
short_name: Exemption for pre-1991 constructed but unused slurry storage systems
label: Exemption for pre-1991 constructed but unused slurry storage systems

proposition_text: These Regulations do not apply to a silo, slurry storage system or fuel storage tank where a contract for its construction, substantial enlargement or substantial reconstruction was entered into before 1st March 1991 and the work was completed before 1st September 1991.
short_name: Exemption for slurry storage systems contracted before March 1991 and completed before September 1991
label: Exemption for slurry storage systems contracted before March 1991 and completed before September 1991

proposition_text: These Regulations apply to a silo, slurry storage system or fuel storage tank otherwise exempt under paragraph (1) if any requirement of a notice under regulation 7 is not complied with within the period stated in the notice.
short_name: Loss of exemption for non-compliance with enforcement notice
label: Loss of exemption for non-compliance with enforcement notice

proposition_text: A person served with a regulation 7 notice may appeal to the Secretary of State within 28 days beginning on the day after the date on which the notice is served, or such longer period as the Secretary of State allows.
short_name: Right to appeal regulation 7 notice within 28 days
label: Right to appeal regulation 7 notice within 28 days

proposition_text: An appeal under regulation 8 must be made by the appellant serving notice on the Secretary of State, and the notice must contain or be accompanied by a statement of the grounds of appeal.
short_name: Appeal procedure requirements
label: Appeal procedure requirements

proposition_text: On determining an appeal under regulation 8, the Secretary of State may direct the Agency to withdraw the regulation 7 notice, modify any of its requirements, extend the period for compliance with any requirement, or dismiss the appeal.
short_name: Secretary of State's powers on determining appeal
label: Secretary of State's powers on determining appeal

proposition_text: The period for compliance with a regulation 7 notice against which an appeal has been made is extended so that it expires on the date on which the Secretary of State finally determines the appeal or, if the appeal is withdrawn, the date on which it is withdrawn, subject to any direction under paragraph (5).
short_name: Automatic extension of compliance period during appeal
label: Automatic extension of compliance period during appeal

proposition_text: Regulation 9 applies to any silo, slurry or fuel oil storage system whose construction is to be begun on or after 15 June 2013.
short_name: Application to new or improved stores (post-15 June 2013)
label: Application to new or improved stores (post-15 June 2013)

proposition_text: A person who proposes to have custody or control of silage, slurry or fuel oil in a new or improved store must give the Agency notice specifying the type of silo or storage system and its location at least 14 days before construction work begins.
short_name: Notice requirement for new or improved stores
label: Notice requirement for new or improved stores

proposition_text: For the purposes of regulation 9, 'construction' includes substantial enlargement and reconstruction.
short_name: Definition of 'construction' for new or improved stores
label: Definition of 'construction' for new or improved stores

proposition_text: A person who contravenes regulation 9 is guilty of an offence and liable on summary conviction to a fine not exceeding level 2 on the standard scale.
short_name: Offence and penalty for contravention of regulation 9
label: Offence and penalty for contravention of regulation 9

proposition_text: The Control of Pollution (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) Regulations 1991 are revoked in so far as they relate to England.
short_name: Revocation of 1991 Regulations (England)
label: Revocation of 1991 Regulations (England)

proposition_text: The Control of Pollution (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (Amendment) Regulations 1997 are revoked in so far as they relate to England.
short_name: Revocation of 1997 Amendment Regulations (England)
label: Revocation of 1997 Amendment Regulations (England)

short_name: Silo base construction and drainage requirements
label: Silo base construction and drainage requirements

proposition_text: The capacity of the effluent tank must not be less than 20 litres for each cubic metre of silo capacity in the case of a silo with a capacity of less than 1,500 cubic metres.
short_name: Effluent tank capacity for silos under 1,500 cubic metres
label: Effluent tank capacity for silos under 1,500 cubic metres

proposition_text: The capacity of the effluent tank must not be less than 30 cubic metres plus 6.7 litres for each cubic metre of silo capacity in excess of 1,500 cubic metres in the case of a silo with a capacity of 1,500 cubic metres or more.

label: Effluent tank capacity for silos of 1,500 cubic metres or more

label: Effluent tank capacity requirement for silos < 1,500 m³

proposition_text: A silo must at no time be loaded to a depth exceeding the maximum depth consistent with the design assumption made in respect of the loadings of the retaining walls.

proposition_text: Notices must be displayed on the retaining walls of a silo in accordance with paragraph 18 of BS 5502: Part 22:2003.

label: Silo retaining wall notice display requirement

proposition_text: A silo, its effluent tank and channels and any pipes must be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to continue to satisfy the requirements of paragraphs 2 to 5 and, if applicable, paragraph 7(a) for at least 20 years, subject to paragraph 9.

proposition_text: An effluent tank with any part below ground level must be designed and constructed to satisfy structural and impermeability requirements for at least 20 years without maintenance.

label: Below-ground effluent tank: 20-year design life without maintenance

proposition_text: The base of a slurry storage tank, the base and walls of any effluent tank, channels and reception pit, and the walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage system components
label: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage system components

proposition_text: The base and walls of the slurry storage tank, any effluent tank, channels and reception pit, and the walls of any pipes must be protected against corrosion in accordance with paragraph 7 of BS 5502: Part 50:1993.
short_name: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry storage system components
label: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry storage system components

proposition_text: Where slurry is proposed to be utilised on the premises by spreading it on the land, the slurry storage tank need not have a greater storage capacity than is adequate to hold the maximum quantity of slurry likely to be produced in any four month period, taking into account specified matters.

short_name: 10-metre setback from waters or Agency-approved precautions

label: Slurry storage system requirements

proposition_text: Channels and reception pits must be impermeable.

proposition_text: The walls of any pipes must be impermeable.

label: Structural load-bearing requirements for slurry storage tanks and reception pits

proposition_text: Facilities used for the temporary storage of slurry before transfer to a slurry storage tank must have adequate capacity to store the maximum quantity of slurry likely to be produced on the premises in any two day period (disregarding slurry transferred directly into a slurry storage tank).
short_name: Temporary slurry storage capacity requirement (two-day maximum)
label: Temporary slurry storage capacity requirement (two-day maximum)

proposition_text: Facilities used for the temporary storage of slurry before transfer to a slurry storage tank may have a lesser capacity than the two-day maximum if the Agency agrees in writing that the lesser capacity is adequate to avoid any significant risk of pollution of controlled waters.
short_name: Temporary slurry storage capacity exception (Agency agreement)
label: Temporary slurry storage capacity exception (Agency agreement)

proposition_text: Where slurry flows into a channel before discharging into a reception pit and the flow of slurry out of the channel is controlled by a sluice, the capacity of the reception pit must be adequate to hold the maximum quantity of slurry that can be released by opening the sluice.
short_name: Reception pit capacity requirement (sluice-controlled channel)
label: Reception pit capacity requirement (sluice-controlled channel)

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank must have adequate storage capacity for the likely quantities of slurry produced from time to time on the premises, taking into account the proposed method of utilising the slurry and the likely rates and times of utilisation, and the matters specified in sub-paragraph (3).
short_name: Adequate storage capacity requirement for slurry storage tanks
label: Adequate storage capacity requirement for slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: Where slurry is to be utilised on the premises by spreading it on the land, the tank need not have a greater storage capacity than is adequate to hold the maximum quantity of slurry likely to be produced in any four month period, taking into account the matters mentioned in sub-paragraph (3).
short_name: Four-month storage capacity exception for on-site land spreading
label: Four-month storage capacity exception for on-site land spreading

proposition_text: In determining adequate storage capacity under sub-paragraphs (1) and (2), account must be taken of the storage capacity of any other slurry storage tank on the premises.
short_name: Consideration of other slurry storage tanks on premises
label: Consideration of other slurry storage tanks on premises

proposition_text: In determining adequate storage capacity under sub-paragraphs (1) and (2), account must be taken of the need to provide at least 750 millimetres of freeboard in the case of a tank with walls made of earth and 300 millimetres of freeboard in all other cases.
short_name: Freeboard requirements for slurry storage tanks
label: Freeboard requirements for slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: No part of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, channels, or reception pit may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters into which slurry could enter if it were to escape, unless precautions agreed in writing by the Agency are taken that are adequate to avoid any significant risk of pollution of controlled waters.
short_name: 10-metre separation distance for slurry storage from waters (unless Agency-agreed precautions)
label: 10-metre separation distance for slurry storage from waters (unless Agency-agreed precautions)

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank and any effluent tank, channels, pipes and reception pit must be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to continue to satisfy the requirements of paragraphs 2 to 4 for at least 20 years.
short_name: Design and construction durability requirement for slurry storage and effluent infrastructure
label: Design and construction durability requirement for slurry storage and effluent infrastructure

proposition_text: If the walls of a slurry storage tank are not impermeable, the base of the tank must extend beyond the walls.
short_name: Base extension requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank walls
label: Base extension requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank walls

proposition_text: If a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, or reception pit is fitted with a drainage pipe, there must be two valves in series on the pipe with each valve separated from the other by a minimum distance of 1 metre, subject to the exception for tanks draining into equal or greater capacity tanks.
short_name: Two-valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry/effluent tanks
label: Two-valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry/effluent tanks

proposition_text: Each valve on a drainage pipe of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, or reception pit must be capable of shutting off the flow of slurry through the pipe.
short_name: Valve capability requirement for slurry drainage systems
label: Valve capability requirement for slurry drainage systems

proposition_text: Each valve on a drainage pipe of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, or reception pit must be kept shut and locked in that position when not in use.
short_name: Valve shut-and-locked requirement when not in use
label: Valve shut-and-locked requirement when not in use

proposition_text: The two-valve requirement does not apply to a slurry storage tank that drains through the pipe into another slurry storage tank if the other tank is of equal or greater capacity or if the tops of the tanks are at the same level.
short_name: Exception to two-valve requirement for inter-tank drainage
label: Exception to two-valve requirement for inter-tank drainage

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank with walls made of earth must not be filled to a level that allows less than 750 millimetres of freeboard.
short_name: Minimum freeboard for earth-walled slurry storage tanks
label: Minimum freeboard for earth-walled slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: A fuel oil storage area with a single fuel storage tank and no other fuel oil stored must be surrounded by a bund capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than 110 per cent of the capacity of the tank.
short_name: Bund capacity requirement for single-tank fuel oil storage area
label: Bund capacity requirement for single-tank fuel oil storage area

proposition_text: The bund and the base of a fuel oil storage area must be impermeable to water and oil and designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to remain so for at least 20 years.
short_name: Impermeability and durability requirements for fuel oil storage bund and base
label: Impermeability and durability requirements for fuel oil storage bund and base

proposition_text: Any tap or valve permanently fixed to a fuel storage tank through which fuel oil can be discharged to the open must be within the bund, arranged to discharge vertically downwards, and shut and locked when not in use.
short_name: Requirements for taps and valves on fuel storage tanks
label: Requirements for taps and valves on fuel storage tanks

proposition_text: No part of the fuel storage area or the bund enclosing it may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters that fuel oil could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum separation distance from waters for fuel oil storage area
label: Minimum separation distance from waters for fuel oil storage area

proposition_text: Requirements must be satisfied in relation to a fuel oil storage area.
short_name: Fuel oil storage area requirements
label: Fuel oil storage area requirements

proposition_text: Where a storage area contains only one fuel storage tank and fuel oil is not otherwise stored there, the bund must be capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than 110 per cent of the capacity of that tank.
short_name: Bund capacity: single tank (110%)
label: Bund capacity: single tank (110%)

proposition_text: Where a storage area contains more than one fuel storage tank and fuel oil is not otherwise stored there, the bund must be capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than the greater of (i) 110 per cent of the capacity of the largest tank or (ii) 25 per cent of the total volume of oil that could be stored in the tanks.
short_name: Bund capacity: multiple tanks (110% largest or 25% total)
label: Bund capacity: multiple tanks (110% largest or 25% total)

proposition_text: Where a storage area contains no fuel storage tank, the bund must be capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than 25 per cent of the total volume of oil at any time stored within the area.
short_name: Bund capacity: no tank (25% of stored volume)
label: Bund capacity: no tank (25% of stored volume)

proposition_text: In any other case, the bund must be capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than the greatest of (i) 110 per cent of the capacity of the largest tank, (ii) if more than one tank, 25 per cent of the total volume that could be stored in the tanks, or (iii) 25 per cent of the total volume of oil at any time stored within the area.
short_name: Bund capacity: other cases (110% largest, 25% tank total, or 25% stored)
label: Bund capacity: other cases (110% largest, 25% tank total, or 25% stored)

proposition_text: The bund and the base of the area must be impermeable to water and oil.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for bund and base
label: Impermeability requirement for bund and base

proposition_text: The bund and the base of the area must be designed and constructed so that they are of sufficient strength and structural integrity so that with proper maintenance they are likely to remain so for at least 20 years.
short_name: Structural integrity and durability requirement for bund and base
label: Structural integrity and durability requirement for bund and base

proposition_text: Every part of any fuel storage tank must be within the bund.
short_name: Fuel storage tank bund containment requirement
label: Fuel storage tank bund containment requirement

proposition_text: Any tap or valve permanently fixed to a fuel storage tank through which fuel oil can be discharged to the open must be within the bund.
short_name: Tap/valve location requirement (within bund)
label: Tap/valve location requirement (within bund)

proposition_text: A flexible pipe permanently attached to a fuel tank through which fuel is delivered must be fitted with a tap or valve at its end that closes automatically when not in use.
short_name: Automatic closure tap/valve requirement for permanently attached flexible fuel delivery pipe
label: Automatic closure tap/valve requirement for permanently attached flexible fuel delivery pipe

proposition_text: No part of the fuel storage area or the bund enclosing it may be situated within 10 metres of any inland freshwaters or coastal waters that fuel oil could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum 10-metre separation distance for fuel storage from waters
label: Minimum 10-metre separation distance for fuel storage from waters

proposition_text: These Regulations may be cited as the Reduction and Prevention of Agricultural Diffuse Pollution (England) Regulations 2018.
short_name: Citation
label: Citation

proposition_text: These Regulations come into force on 2nd April 2018.
short_name: Commencement date
label: Commencement date

proposition_text: These Regulations extend to England and Wales.
short_name: Territorial extent
label: Territorial extent

proposition_text: These Regulations apply to agricultural land in England.
short_name: Territorial application
label: Territorial application

proposition_text: A land manager must not apply organic manure or manufactured fertiliser to agricultural land if the soil is waterlogged, flooded or snow covered.
short_name: Prohibition on applying manure/fertiliser to waterlogged, flooded or snow covered soil
label: Prohibition on applying manure/fertiliser to waterlogged, flooded or snow covered soil

proposition_text: A land manager must ensure that each application of organic manure or manufactured fertiliser to agricultural land is planned so that it does not exceed the needs of the soil and crop on that land or give rise to a significant risk of agricultural diffuse pollution.
short_name: Planning requirement for organic manure and manufactured fertiliser applications
label: Planning requirement for organic manure and manufactured fertiliser applications

proposition_text: A land manager must ensure that each application of organic manure or manufactured fertiliser to agricultural land takes into account the weather conditions and forecasts for that land at the time of the application.
short_name: Weather conditions consideration requirement
label: Weather conditions consideration requirement

proposition_text: When planning to avoid significant risk of agricultural diffuse pollution, a land manager must ensure that factors including slope of the land (particularly if greater than 12 degrees), ground cover, proximity to inland freshwaters, coastal waters, wetlands, or to a spring, well or borehole, soil type and condition, and presence and condition of agricultural land drains are taken into account.
short_name: Risk factors to be considered in planning
label: Risk factors to be considered in planning

proposition_text: In addition to planning requirements, a land manager must ensure that reasonable precautions are taken to prevent agricultural diffuse pollution resulting from applications of organic manure or manufactured fertiliser.
short_name: General reasonable precautions duty
label: General reasonable precautions duty

proposition_text: When planning an application of organic manure or fertiliser to cultivated agricultural land, a land manager must ensure that the results of soil sampling and analysis are taken into account.
short_name: Duty to consider soil sampling results when planning applications to cultivated land
label: Duty to consider soil sampling results when planning applications to cultivated land

proposition_text: Soil sampling and analysis results must include the pH of the soil and the levels of nitrogen, phosphorous, magnesium and potassium present, must be no more than 5 years old at the time of application, and may have been collected before these Regulations came into force or by another land manager.
short_name: Requirements for soil sampling and analysis results
label: Requirements for soil sampling and analysis results

proposition_text: Nitrogen levels may be determined by means of assessment of the soil nitrogen supply, rather than by sampling and analysis of soil.
short_name: Alternative method for determining nitrogen levels
label: Alternative method for determining nitrogen levels

proposition_text: Cultivated agricultural land means agricultural land which has been cultivated by physical means (including ploughing, sowing or harvesting) at least once in the previous year, or by chemical means (including the application of organic manure or manufactured fertiliser) at least once in the previous 3 years.
short_name: Definition of cultivated agricultural land
label: Definition of cultivated agricultural land

proposition_text: A land manager must ensure that manufactured fertiliser is not applied to agricultural land within two metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters, or of a spring, well or borehole.
short_name: Prohibition on applying manufactured fertiliser within two metres of water bodies
label: Prohibition on applying manufactured fertiliser within two metres of water bodies

proposition_text: A land manager must not apply organic manure to agricultural land within 10 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters.
short_name: Organic manure: 10-metre buffer zone from waters
label: Organic manure: 10-metre buffer zone from waters

proposition_text: Where precision spreading equipment is used, organic manure must not be applied within 6 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters.
short_name: Precision spreading: 6-metre buffer zone
label: Precision spreading: 6-metre buffer zone

proposition_text: Livestock manure (other than slurry or poultry manure) may be applied to land managed for breeding wader birds or species-rich semi-natural grassland subject to agri-environmental commitment or SSSI notification, during 1 June to 31 October, provided it is not applied directly onto waters and does not exceed 12.5 tonnes per hectare annually.
short_name: Livestock manure exception: breeding wader / semi-natural grassland
label: Livestock manure exception: breeding wader / semi-natural grassland

proposition_text: "Agri-environmental commitment" means any commitment entered into under Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 or Regulation (EU) No. 1305/2013 on support for rural development by the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development.
short_name: Definition: agri-environmental commitment
label: Definition: agri-environmental commitment

proposition_text: A land manager must ensure that organic manure is not applied to agricultural land within 50 metres of a spring, well or borehole.
short_name: Prohibition on applying organic manure within 50 metres of water sources
label: Prohibition on applying organic manure within 50 metres of water sources

proposition_text: A land manager must not store organic manure on agricultural land within 10 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters.
short_name: Prohibition on storing organic manure within 10 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters
label: Prohibition on storing organic manure within 10 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters

proposition_text: A land manager must not store organic manure on agricultural land within 50 metres of a spring, well or borehole.
short_name: Prohibition on storing organic manure within 50 metres of springs, wells or boreholes
label: Prohibition on storing organic manure within 50 metres of springs, wells or boreholes

proposition_text: For agricultural land not subject to the distance restrictions in regulation 9(1), a land manager must take into account any factors creating a significant risk of agricultural diffuse pollution when deciding where to store organic manure.
short_name: Risk assessment requirement for organic manure storage on other agricultural land
label: Risk assessment requirement for organic manure storage on other agricultural land

proposition_text: The factors to be considered under regulation 9(2) include those specified in regulation 4(2).
short_name: Cross-reference to factors in regulation 4(2) for organic manure storage risk assessment
label: Cross-reference to factors in regulation 4(2) for organic manure storage risk assessment

proposition_text: A land manager must prevent poaching within 5 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters.
short_name: Prohibition of poaching near water bodies
label: Prohibition of poaching near water bodies

proposition_text: A land manager must not position a livestock feeder within 10 metres of inland freshwaters or coastal waters.
short_name: Livestock feeder setback from water bodies
label: Livestock feeder setback from water bodies

proposition_text: A land manager must not position a livestock feeder within 50 metres of a spring, well or borehole.
short_name: Livestock feeder setback from groundwater sources
label: Livestock feeder setback from groundwater sources

proposition_text: A land manager must take reasonable precautions to prevent agricultural diffuse pollution resulting from land management and cultivation practices on agricultural land.
short_name: General duty to prevent diffuse pollution from land practices
label: General duty to prevent diffuse pollution from land practices

proposition_text: A person who fails to comply with the requirements of any of regulations 3 to 10 commits an offence.
short_name: Offence for non-compliance with regulations 3 to 10
label: Offence for non-compliance with regulations 3 to 10

proposition_text: A person guilty of an offence under regulation 11 is liable, either on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment, to a fine.
short_name: Penalty for offence under regulation 11
label: Penalty for offence under regulation 11

proposition_text: Where a body corporate is guilty of an offence under regulation 11 and the offence is proved to have been committed with the consent or connivance of an officer, or to have been attributable to any neglect on the part of an officer, the officer as well as the body corporate is guilty of the offence and liable to be proceeded against and punished accordingly.
short_name: Officer liability for corporate offences
label: Officer liability for corporate offences

proposition_text: In regulation 11(3), 'officer' means a director, manager, secretary or other similar officer of the body, or a person purporting to act in any such capacity.
short_name: Definition of 'officer' for corporate liability
label: Definition of 'officer' for corporate liability

proposition_text: For an offence under regulation 11(1), the Agency may impose a restoration notice, compliance notice, stop notice, fixed monetary penalty, variable monetary penalty or accept an enforcement undertaking as if it were an offence specified in Schedule 5 to the Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010.
short_name: Agency enforcement powers for regulation 11(1) offences
label: Agency enforcement powers for regulation 11(1) offences

proposition_text: An expression used in regulation 13(1) and in the Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010 has the same meaning in regulation 13(1) as it has in that Order.
short_name: Interpretive alignment with Environmental Civil Sanctions Order
label: Interpretive alignment with Environmental Civil Sanctions Order

proposition_text: The provisions of the Environmental Civil Sanctions (England) Order 2010 relating to the sanctions in regulation 13(1) apply as if they were provisions of these Regulations.
short_name: Incorporation of Environmental Civil Sanctions Order provisions
label: Incorporation of Environmental Civil Sanctions Order provisions

proposition_text: The Agency has the function of enforcing these Regulations.
short_name: Agency enforcement function
label: Agency enforcement function

proposition_text: The Secretary of State may issue guidance to the Agency with respect to the exercise of the Agency's functions under these Regulations.
short_name: Power to issue guidance to Agency
label: Power to issue guidance to Agency

proposition_text: In the exercise of its functions, the Agency must have regard to any guidance issued by the Secretary of State under regulation 15(1).
short_name: Agency duty to have regard to guidance
label: Agency duty to have regard to guidance

proposition_text: The Secretary of State must publish any guidance issued under regulation 15(1) on a website maintained by or on behalf of the Secretary of State.
short_name: Duty to publish guidance on website
label: Duty to publish guidance on website

proposition_text: These Regulations may be cited as the Control of Pollution (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 and shall come into force on 1st December 2003.
short_name: Citation and commencement
label: Citation and commencement

proposition_text: Slurry means excreta, including any liquid fraction, produced by livestock whilst in a yard or building; or a mixture consisting wholly of or containing such excreta, bedding, feed residues, rainwater and washings from a building or yard used by livestock, dungsteads or middens, high level slatted buildings and weeping wall structures or any combination of these, provided such excreta is present, of a consistency that allows it to be pumped or discharged by gravity at any stage in the handling process.
short_name: Definition of slurry
label: Definition of slurry

proposition_text: Slurry storage system means a slurry storage tank; any reception pit and any effluent tank used in connection with the slurry storage tank; and any channels and pipes used in connection with the slurry storage tank, any reception pit or any effluent tank.
short_name: Definition of slurry storage system
label: Definition of slurry storage system

proposition_text: These Regulations shall extend to Scotland only.
short_name: Territorial extent
label: Territorial extent

proposition_text: A person having custody or control of a crop being made into silage or of silage being stored must keep it in a silo that satisfies Schedule 1 requirements or is an exempt structure under regulation 5.
short_name: Silage storage in compliant or exempt silo
label: Silage storage in compliant or exempt silo

proposition_text: A person having custody or control of silage made in the form of wrapped and sealed bales must not open or remove the wrapping unless done at a place at least 10 metres from any inland or coastal waters.
short_name: Prohibition on opening wrapped silage bales near waters
label: Prohibition on opening wrapped silage bales near waters

proposition_text: A person having custody or control of slurry must store it in a slurry storage system that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 2 or is an exempt structure by virtue of regulation 5.
short_name: Slurry storage system requirement
label: Slurry storage system requirement

proposition_text: The slurry storage system requirement does not apply to slurry while it is stored temporarily in a tanker with a capacity not exceeding 18,000 litres which is used for transporting slurry on roads or about a farm.
short_name: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers
label: Exception for temporary storage in transport tankers

proposition_text: No person shall have custody or control of fuel oil on a farm unless it is stored in a fuel storage tank or container within a storage area that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 3.
short_name: Fuel oil storage in compliant tank/container
label: Fuel oil storage in compliant tank/container

proposition_text: Fuel oil on a farm may be stored in drums within a storage area that satisfies the requirements of Schedule 3.
short_name: Fuel oil storage in drums
label: Fuel oil storage in drums

proposition_text: Fuel oil on a farm may be stored temporarily in a tanker used for transporting fuel oil on roads or about the farm.
short_name: Temporary fuel oil storage in tanker
label: Temporary fuel oil storage in tanker

proposition_text: The prohibition on fuel oil storage does not apply if the total quantity of fuel oil stored on the farm does not exceed 1,250 litres.
short_name: De minimis exemption for fuel oil storage
label: De minimis exemption for fuel oil storage

proposition_text: A silo, slurry storage system, fuel storage tank or container is an exempt structure if it was an exempt structure under the 2001 Regulations and had not ceased to be exempt immediately before these Regulations came into force, and it does not cease to be exempt by virtue of regulation 7(1).
short_name: Transitional exemption for structures exempt under 2001 Regulations
label: Transitional exemption for structures exempt under 2001 Regulations

proposition_text: Regulation 2 does not apply where a person makes bulk bagged silage on a farm in bags that are made of 1000 gauge polyethylene or material of at least equivalent impermeability and durability, are kept sealed to prevent the escape of silage effluent, and are stored on a firm level surface and incorporate a facility designed to enable the safe removal of excess effluent when present.
short_name: Exemption from regulation 2 for bulk bagged silage meeting specified standards
label: Exemption from regulation 2 for bulk bagged silage meeting specified standards

proposition_text: A person is not entitled to rely on the exemption for bulk bagged silage unless that person stores any bags in which silage is being made or stored at a place at least 10 metres from any inland or coastal waters.
short_name: 10-metre distance requirement for bulk bagged silage storage
label: 10-metre distance requirement for bulk bagged silage storage

proposition_text: No person having custody or control of any crop which has been made into silage in bulk bags shall open or empty any bag in which silage has been made unless that person does so at a place at least 10 metres from any inland or coastal waters.
short_name: 10-metre distance requirement for opening or emptying bulk bagged silage
label: 10-metre distance requirement for opening or emptying bulk bagged silage

proposition_text: An exempt structure shall cease to be exempt if any requirement of a notice under regulation 8(1) is not complied with within the period stated in the notice.
short_name: Loss of exemption for non-compliance with notice
label: Loss of exemption for non-compliance with notice

proposition_text: References to the period stated in a notice include that period as extended under regulation 8(5) or by virtue of regulation 9(5) or (6), and references to a requirement of a notice include that requirement as modified under regulation 8(5) or by virtue of regulation 9(5).
short_name: Interpretation of notice periods and requirements
label: Interpretation of notice periods and requirements

proposition_text: SEPA may serve notice on any person with custody or control of any relevant substance requiring that person to carry out works, take precautions, or take other steps to reduce to a minimum any significant risk of pollution of inland or coastal waters.
short_name: SEPA power to serve notice requiring pollution prevention measures
label: SEPA power to serve notice requiring pollution prevention measures

proposition_text: A notice served under regulation 8 must specify or describe the required works, precautions or steps, state the compliance period, and inform the recipient of the effect of regulation 9.
short_name: Mandatory content of SEPA notice
label: Mandatory content of SEPA notice

proposition_text: The works, precautions or steps specified in a notice may include the requirement to establish and implement a Farm Waste Management Plan in accordance with the code of practice approved under section 51(1) of the Control of Pollution Act 1974.
short_name: Notice may require Farm Waste Management Plan
label: Notice may require Farm Waste Management Plan

proposition_text: The compliance period stated in a notice must be reasonable in the circumstances and must not end earlier than 28 days from the day the notice is served.
short_name: Minimum 28-day compliance period for notice
label: Minimum 28-day compliance period for notice

proposition_text: A person served with a notice under regulation 8 may appeal to the Scottish Ministers within 28 days beginning with the day on which that notice is served, or within such longer period as the Scottish Ministers may allow.
short_name: Right to appeal regulation 8 notice within 28 days
label: Right to appeal regulation 8 notice within 28 days

proposition_text: An appeal under regulation 9 shall be made by the appellant serving notice on the Scottish Ministers, and the notice shall contain or be accompanied by a statement of the grounds of appeal.
short_name: Appeal procedure requires notice with grounds of appeal
label: Appeal procedure requires notice with grounds of appeal

proposition_text: On determining an appeal under regulation 9, the Scottish Ministers may direct SEPA to withdraw the notice, extend the period for compliance with any requirement of the notice, modify any requirement of the notice, or dismiss the appeal.
short_name: Scottish Ministers' powers on determining appeal
label: Scottish Ministers' powers on determining appeal

proposition_text: The period for compliance with a notice under regulation 8 shall be extended so that it expires on the date on which the Scottish Ministers finally determine the appeal or, if the appeal is withdrawn, the date on which it is withdrawn.
short_name: Automatic extension of compliance period pending appeal determination
label: Automatic extension of compliance period pending appeal determination

proposition_text: A person who proposes to have custody or control of any relevant substance which is to be kept or stored on a farm in a silo, slurry storage system or fuel storage area constructed, substantially enlarged or substantially reconstructed on or after 29th December 2003 shall serve notice on SEPA specifying the type of structure to be used and its location at least 28 days before it is to be used for such purpose.
short_name: Notice to SEPA for new or substantially altered storage structures
label: Notice to SEPA for new or substantially altered storage structures

proposition_text: A person who contravenes regulation 2(1), 2(2), 3(1), 4(1), 6 or 10 commits an offence.
short_name: Offence for contravention of specified regulations
label: Offence for contravention of specified regulations

proposition_text: On summary conviction for contravening regulation 2(1), 2(2), 3(1), 4(1), 6 or 10, a person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding three months or to a fine not exceeding £20,000 or to both.
short_name: Penalty on summary conviction
label: Penalty on summary conviction

proposition_text: On conviction on indictment for contravening regulation 2(1), 2(2), 3(1), 4(1), 6 or 10, a person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or to a fine or to both.
short_name: Penalty on conviction on indictment
label: Penalty on conviction on indictment

proposition_text: The Control of Pollution (Silage, Slurry and Agricultural Fuel Oil) (Scotland) Regulations 1999 (insofar as they remain in force) and the 2001 Regulations are revoked.
short_name: Revocation of 1999 and 2001 Regulations
label: Revocation of 1999 and 2001 Regulations

proposition_text: The base of a silo with retaining walls made other than of earth must extend beyond those walls, and in all cases the base must be provided with channels constructed to collect any silage effluent which may escape from the silo, with adequate provision for drainage of that effluent from the channels to an effluent tank through a channel or pipe.
short_name: Silo base construction and effluent collection requirements
label: Silo base construction and effluent collection requirements

proposition_text: For a silo with capacity less than 1500 cubic metres, the effluent tank capacity must be not less than 3 cubic metres for each 150 cubic metres or part thereof of silo capacity.
short_name: Effluent tank capacity for silos under 1500 cubic metres
label: Effluent tank capacity for silos under 1500 cubic metres

proposition_text: For a silo with capacity of 1500 cubic metres or more, the effluent tank capacity must be not less than 30 cubic metres plus 1 cubic metre for each 150 cubic metres or part thereof of silo capacity in excess of 1500 cubic metres.
short_name: Effluent tank capacity for silos of 1500 cubic metres or more
label: Effluent tank capacity for silos of 1500 cubic metres or more

proposition_text: No part of the silo, its effluent tank, channels, or any associated pipes may be situated within 10 metres of any inland or coastal waters which silage effluent could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum separation distance from waters
label: Minimum separation distance from waters

proposition_text: A silo must comply with the provisions of this Schedule or be designed and constructed in accordance with BS 5502 (parts relating to Cylindrical Forage Towers).
short_name: Silo compliance requirements
label: Silo compliance requirements

proposition_text: Where a silo has retaining walls made other than of earth, the base of the silo shall extend beyond those walls.
short_name: Silo base extension requirement (non-earth walls)
label: Silo base extension requirement (non-earth walls)

proposition_text: The base of the silo shall in all cases be provided with channels so constructed as to collect any silage effluent which may escape from the silo.
short_name: Silo base channels for effluent collection
label: Silo base channels for effluent collection

proposition_text: Adequate provision shall be made for the drainage of silage effluent from the channels to an effluent tank through a channel or pipe.
short_name: Drainage provision from channels to effluent tank
label: Drainage provision from channels to effluent tank

proposition_text: For a silo with a capacity of less than 1500 cubic metres, the effluent tank capacity shall be not less than 3 cubic metres for each 150 cubic metres or part thereof of silo capacity.
short_name: Effluent tank capacity for silos < 1500 m³
label: Effluent tank capacity for silos < 1500 m³

proposition_text: For a silo with a capacity of 1500 cubic metres or more, the effluent tank capacity shall be not less than 30 cubic metres plus 1 cubic metre for each 150 cubic metres or part thereof of silo capacity in excess of 1500 cubic metres.
short_name: Effluent tank capacity for silos ≥ 1500 m³
label: Effluent tank capacity for silos ≥ 1500 m³

proposition_text: The effluent collection system associated with silos may, with the agreement of SEPA, incorporate a system of pumps and sumps, together with detailed sizing, pumping and management requirements, designed to reduce the capacity of the effluent tank.
short_name: Alternative effluent system with SEPA agreement
label: Alternative effluent system with SEPA agreement

proposition_text: The base of a silo, the base and walls of its effluent tank and channels, and the walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for silo bases, effluent tanks, channels and pipes
label: Impermeability requirement for silo bases, effluent tanks, channels and pipes

proposition_text: The base and any walls of the silo, its effluent tank and channels and the walls of any pipes shall, so far as reasonably practicable, be resistant to attack by silage effluent.
short_name: Resistance to silage effluent attack
label: Resistance to silage effluent attack

proposition_text: Where the walls of the silo, effluent tank, channels or pipes are made of earth, they shall be lined with an impermeable membrane.
short_name: Impermeable membrane lining for earth walls
label: Impermeable membrane lining for earth walls

proposition_text: No part of a silage silo, its effluent tank, channels, or any associated pipes shall be situated within 10 metres of any inland or coastal waters which silage effluent could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum 10-metre separation distance for silage infrastructure from waters
label: Minimum 10-metre separation distance for silage infrastructure from waters

proposition_text: Retaining walls of a silo must be capable of withstanding minimum wall loadings calculated on the assumptions and in the manner indicated by paragraph 15.6.1 to 15.6.3 of BS 5502 (Part 22: 1993).
short_name: Retaining wall strength requirement for silos
label: Retaining wall strength requirement for silos

proposition_text: A silo with retaining walls must not at any time be loaded to a depth exceeding the maximum depth consistent with the design assumption made in respect of the loadings of the retaining walls.
short_name: Maximum loading depth restriction for silos with retaining walls
label: Maximum loading depth restriction for silos with retaining walls

proposition_text: A silo, its effluent tank and channels and any pipes must be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to satisfy the requirements of paragraphs 2 to 5 and, if applicable, 7(a) for a period of at least 20 years, subject to paragraph 9.
short_name: Design and construction durability requirement for silos and effluent infrastructure
label: Design and construction durability requirement for silos and effluent infrastructure

proposition_text: Any part of an effluent tank installed below ground level shall be designed and constructed in accordance with BS 5502 so that with proper maintenance it is likely to satisfy the requirements of paragraphs 4 and 5 for a period of at least 20 years.
short_name: Below-ground effluent tank design and construction standard
label: Below-ground effluent tank design and construction standard

proposition_text: The base of a slurry storage tank, the base and walls of any effluent tank, channels and reception pit, and the walls of any pipes must be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage system components
label: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage system components

proposition_text: The base and walls of slurry storage tanks, effluent tanks, channels, reception pits, and pipe walls must be protected against corrosion in accordance with paragraph 7.2 of BS 5502 (Part 50: 1993).
short_name: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry storage system components
label: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry storage system components

proposition_text: No part of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, channels, pipes, or reception pit may be situated within 10 metres of any inland or coastal waters which slurry could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum separation distance from waters for slurry storage systems
label: Minimum separation distance from waters for slurry storage systems

proposition_text: Where a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, or reception pit is fitted with a drainage pipe, there must be two valves in series on the pipe, each capable of stopping slurry flow and kept shut and locked when not in use, unless the tank drains into another slurry storage tank of equal or greater capacity or where the tops of the tanks are at the same level.
short_name: Dual valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry storage systems
label: Dual valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry storage systems

proposition_text: A slurry storage system must satisfy specified requirements.
short_name: Slurry storage system requirements
label: Slurry storage system requirements

proposition_text: The base of a slurry storage tank shall be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage tank base
label: Impermeability requirement for slurry storage tank base

proposition_text: The base and walls of any effluent tank, channels and reception pit shall be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for effluent tank, channels and reception pit
label: Impermeability requirement for effluent tank, channels and reception pit

proposition_text: The walls of any pipes used for slurry or effluent shall be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for pipe walls
label: Impermeability requirement for pipe walls

proposition_text: The base and walls of slurry storage tanks, effluent tanks, channels, reception pits, and the walls of pipes must be protected against corrosion in accordance with paragraph 7.2 of BS 5502 (Part 50: 1993).
short_name: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry and effluent storage infrastructure
label: Corrosion protection requirement for slurry and effluent storage infrastructure

proposition_text: The base and walls of a slurry storage tank and any reception pit must be capable of withstanding characteristic loads calculated on the assumptions and in the manner indicated by paragraph 5 of the Code of Practice.
short_name: Structural capacity requirement for slurry storage tank and reception pit
label: Structural capacity requirement for slurry storage tank and reception pit

proposition_text: Facilities used for the temporary storage of slurry before transfer to a slurry storage tank must have adequate capacity to store the maximum quantity of slurry likely to be produced on a farm in any two day period.
short_name: Temporary slurry storage capacity requirement (two-day rule)
label: Temporary slurry storage capacity requirement (two-day rule)

proposition_text: Where slurry flows into a channel before discharging into a reception pit and the flow is controlled by a sluice, the capacity of the reception pit must be adequate to store the maximum quantity of slurry which can be released by opening the sluice.
short_name: Reception pit capacity requirement for sluice-controlled channels
label: Reception pit capacity requirement for sluice-controlled channels

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank must have adequate storage capacity for the likely quantities of slurry produced from time to time on the farm, having regard to the proposed method of disposal (including likely rates and times of disposal) and specified matters.
short_name: Adequate slurry storage capacity requirement
label: Adequate slurry storage capacity requirement

proposition_text: Where slurry is to be disposed of on a farm by spreading it on the land, the tank need not have a greater storage capacity than is adequate to store the maximum quantity of slurry likely to be produced in any continuous six month period, having regard to specified matters.
short_name: Six-month storage capacity limit for land-spreading disposal
label: Six-month storage capacity limit for land-spreading disposal

proposition_text: In determining adequate slurry storage capacity, regard must be had to the storage capacity of any other slurry storage tank on the premises.
short_name: Consideration of other slurry storage tanks on premises
label: Consideration of other slurry storage tanks on premises

proposition_text: In determining adequate slurry storage capacity, regard must be had to the need to provide not less than 750 millimetres of freeboard for tanks with earth walls and 300 millimetres of freeboard in all other cases.
short_name: Freeboard requirements for slurry storage tanks
label: Freeboard requirements for slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: No part of a slurry storage tank, effluent tank, channels, pipes, or reception pit may be situated within 10 metres of any inland or coastal waters which slurry could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum 10-metre separation distance for slurry infrastructure from waters
label: Minimum 10-metre separation distance for slurry infrastructure from waters

proposition_text: Slurry storage tanks, effluent tanks, channels, pipes and reception pits must be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to satisfy structural and impermeability requirements for at least 20 years.
short_name: Design life requirement for slurry and effluent storage infrastructure
label: Design life requirement for slurry and effluent storage infrastructure

proposition_text: Where the walls of a slurry storage tank are not impermeable, the base of the tank must extend beyond its walls.
short_name: Base extension requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank walls
label: Base extension requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank walls

proposition_text: Where the walls of a slurry storage tank are not impermeable, the base must be provided with channels designed and constructed to collect any slurry which may escape from the tank.
short_name: Collection channel requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank base
label: Collection channel requirement for non-impermeable slurry tank base

proposition_text: Adequate provision must be made for the drainage of slurry from the collection channels to an effluent tank through a channel or pipe.
short_name: Drainage provision requirement from collection channels to effluent tank
label: Drainage provision requirement from collection channels to effluent tank

proposition_text: Where a slurry storage tank, effluent tank or reception pit is fitted with a drainage pipe, there must be two valves in series on the pipe, each capable of stopping the flow of slurry, and each valve must be kept shut and locked when not in use.
short_name: Two-valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry/effluent tanks
label: Two-valve requirement for drainage pipes on slurry/effluent tanks

proposition_text: The two-valve requirement does not apply to a slurry storage tank which drains through the pipe into another slurry storage tank of equal or greater capacity or where the tops of the tanks are at the same level.
short_name: Exception to two-valve requirement for inter-tank drainage
label: Exception to two-valve requirement for inter-tank drainage

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank with walls made of earth must not be filled to a level that allows less than 750 millimetres of freeboard.
short_name: Freeboard requirement for earth-walled slurry storage tanks
label: Freeboard requirement for earth-walled slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: A slurry storage tank that does not have walls made of earth must not be filled to a level that allows less than 300 millimetres of freeboard.
short_name: Freeboard requirement for non-earth-walled slurry storage tanks
label: Freeboard requirement for non-earth-walled slurry storage tanks

proposition_text: A fuel oil storage area must be surrounded by a bund capable of retaining at least 110% of the capacity of the largest tank (where one tank exists) or the greater of 110% of the largest tank or 25% of total storage volume (where multiple tanks exist).
short_name: Bund retention capacity for fuel oil storage areas
label: Bund retention capacity for fuel oil storage areas

proposition_text: The bund and base of a fuel oil storage area must be impermeable and designed to remain so for at least 20 years with proper maintenance.
short_name: Impermeability and durability requirements for fuel oil bunds
label: Impermeability and durability requirements for fuel oil bunds

proposition_text: Any tap or valve permanently fixed to a fuel storage tank through which fuel oil can be discharged must be within the bund, discharge vertically downwards, and be shut and locked when not in use.
short_name: Requirements for taps and valves on fuel storage tanks
label: Requirements for taps and valves on fuel storage tanks

proposition_text: No part of a fuel oil storage area or its bund may be situated within 10 metres of any inland or coastal waters which fuel oil could enter if it escaped.
short_name: Minimum separation distance from waters for fuel oil storage
label: Minimum separation distance from waters for fuel oil storage

proposition_text: Requirements must be satisfied in relation to a fuel oil storage area.
short_name: Fuel oil storage area requirements
label: Fuel oil storage area requirements

proposition_text: A fuel oil storage area containing all fuel oil in one fuel storage tank shall be surrounded by a bund capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than 110 per cent of the capacity of the tank.
short_name: Bund capacity requirement for single fuel storage tank
label: Bund capacity requirement for single fuel storage tank

proposition_text: A fuel oil storage area containing all fuel oil in more than one fuel storage tank shall be surrounded by a bund capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than the greater of: (i) 110 per cent of the capacity of the largest tank within the storage area; or (ii) 25 per cent of the total volume of such oil which could be stored in tanks within the area.
short_name: Bund capacity requirement for multiple fuel storage tanks
label: Bund capacity requirement for multiple fuel storage tanks

proposition_text: A fuel oil storage area in any other case shall be surrounded by a bund capable of retaining a volume of fuel oil not less than the greater of: (i) 110 per cent of the capacity of the largest container within the storage area; or (ii) 25 per cent of the total volume of such oil at any time stored within the area.
short_name: Bund capacity requirement for fuel oil storage (other cases)
label: Bund capacity requirement for fuel oil storage (other cases)

proposition_text: The bund and the base of the storage area for slurry, manure or agricultural effluent shall be impermeable.
short_name: Impermeability requirement for bund and base
label: Impermeability requirement for bund and base

proposition_text: The bund and the base of the storage area for slurry, manure or agricultural effluent shall be designed and constructed so that with proper maintenance they are likely to remain impermeable for a period of at least 20 years.
short_name: Design and construction durability requirement
label: Design and construction durability requirement

proposition_text: Every part of any fuel storage tank shall be within the bund.
short_name: Fuel storage tank containment requirement
label: Fuel storage tank containment requirement

proposition_text: Any tap or valve permanently fixed to a fuel oil tank through which fuel oil can be discharged to the open must be located within the bund.
short_name: Tap/valve location requirement (within bund)
label: Tap/valve location requirement (within bund)

proposition_text: Any tap or valve permanently fixed to a fuel oil tank through which fuel oil can be discharged to the open must be arranged to discharge vertically downwards.
short_name: Tap/valve discharge direction requirement
label: Tap/valve discharge direction requirement

proposition_text: Any tap or valve permanently fixed to a fuel oil tank through which fuel oil can be discharged to the open must be shut and locked when not in use.
short_name: Tap/valve shut and locked requirement
label: Tap/valve shut and locked requirement

proposition_text: Where fuel from a tank is discharged through a permanently attached flexible pipe, the pipe shall be fitted with a tap or valve at its end which closes automatically when not in use.
short_name: Automatic closure tap/valve required on permanently attached flexible fuel discharge pipe
label: Automatic closure tap/valve required on permanently attached flexible fuel discharge pipe

proposition_text: No part of a fuel oil storage area or its enclosing bund may be situated within 10 metres of any inland or coastal waters which fuel oil could enter if it were to escape.
short_name: Minimum separation distance for fuel oil storage from waters
label: Minimum separation distance for fuel oil storage from waters
